# Grid General (Insular Imperium)

> A turn-based strategy game set in a Roman/medieval island-warfare setting, built with Phaser 3 and JavaScript (ESM). Players command armies on procedurally generated islands, fighting Orc factions and a Red AI opponent in grid-based tactical combat with naval warfare.

## Tech Stack

- **Game framework:** Phaser 3 (v3.90.0) — rendering, sprites, scenes, input, animations, game loop
- **Audio:** Tone.js — procedural sound effects (hit, heal, level-up, frenzy, etc.)
- **Language:** JavaScript ES Modules (`"type": "module"` in package.json)
- **Desktop packaging:** Electron + electron-builder (Windows/macOS/Linux)
- **Dev server:** Vite (HMR, on-demand serving)
- **Compression:** lz-string (save data)
- **Tile art:** 16×16 MiniWorld sprites with blob-based autotiling

## Project Structure

```
main.js                  — Phaser game bootstrap (creates Phaser.Game with scene list)
index.html               — Entry point, importmap for ESM CDN deps, game container
config.js                — Central config: tile size, map dimensions, economy, build costs,
                           UI layout, modal panel keys, teams, active modes, AI params,
                           terrain, ship classes, unit stats, map generation, PRODUCTION
                           (FORT_SIZE_UNIT_KEYS, PORT_SHIP_KEYS for fort/port build restrictions)
GameState.js             — createGameState() factory: plain object holding all mutable state
                           (units, selection, reachable tiles, combat targets, turn, gold, UI flags)
AudioManager.js          — Tone.js procedural audio (hit, heal, level-up, siege sounds)
MapManager.js            — Map orchestrator: delegates to terrain generator, location placer,
                           pathing service; holds grid, locations, bitmask computation
MapTerrainGenerator.js   — Procedural island generation (water edges, inlets, terrain types)
MapLocationPlacer.js     — Places forts, orc camps, ports, workshops with name pools
MapPathingService.js     — A* pathing (findPath optional options.units/movingUnit to avoid occupied tiles),
                           getReachableTiles (excludes occupied), road generation via MST, bridge placement

scenes/
  MainMenuScene.js       — Title screen, New Game / Load Game / Island Select buttons
  IslandSelectScene.js   — Placeholder for island/faction/difficulty selection
  GameScene.js           — Core gameplay scene (~617 lines): initializes 13+ managers,
                           creates map display, manages turn flow

entities/
  Unit.js                — Base entity class (Phaser Container): HP, ATK, DEF, MOV, level/XP,
                           multi-turn pathing (persistentPath), moveTo, moveAlongPath (step-by-step;
                           resolves with { blocked, stepsCompleted } if next tile occupied), embark
  Hero.js                — Extends Unit: named hero with bonus stats, passive aura (4-tile range),
                           active ability with cooldown, golden badge, portrait, aura ring visuals
  Orc.js                 — Extends Unit: Frenzy mechanic (+50% ATK at ≤50% HP)
  OrcHealer.js           — Orc healer subtype
  OrcMage.js             — Orc mage subtype
  OrcRider.js            — Orc mounted subtype
  OrcSpear.js            — Orc spear subtype

managers/
  AIManager.js           — Enemy turn AI for Red and Orc; camera startFollow(unit) during each unit's move
  AnimationManager.js    — Unit animations, visual effects; mage spell (lightning) — undead mage uses bright green
  CameraManager.js       — Map scrolling, zoom, tile cursors (including road-placement cursor)
  CombatManager.js       — Attack resolution, flanking, damage calculation, stat breakdowns
  EconomyManager.js      — Gold income, maintenance, loot, purchase transactions
  EngineerManager.js     — Workshop/road/bridge/castra construction, engineer staffing
  HeroManager.js         — Hero selection, aura application, ability activation (9-hero roster)
  InputManager.js        — Click/keyboard handling, selection; path for immediate move uses unit-avoidance;
                           distant path (Path Move) ignores units; blocked result → "Blocked" message
  NavalManager.js        — Ship lifecycle facade, delegates to 7 naval sub-modules
  ProductionManager.js   — Production queues; getBuildableUnitKeys/getBuildableShipKeys by fort size/port
                           (CONFIG.PRODUCTION); red fort spawn and blue completion validate buildable roster
  SaveManager.js         — Serialization/deserialization with swappable storage backends
  SiegeManager.js        — Siege unit logic (catapults/ballistae) — stub/WIP
  SpawnManager.js        — Initial army deployment and unit spawning
  TurnManager.js         — Turn cycling (Blue → Red → Orc), Path Move / Move All (camera follows
                           moving unit), processAutoMovePaths (blocked handling, persistent path trim)
  UIManager.js           — HUD facade: delegates to managers/ui/ (MedievalUI, MapHighlightUI,
                           ManageCrewPanel, StatPanelUI, ProductionReportUI, ShipPlacementConfirmUI)

managers/naval/
  NavalAI.js             — AI ship turn logic (aggro, movement, bombard targeting)
  ShipCombat.js          — Ship bombard targeting, execution, damage to ships/units/locations
  ShipCrew.js            — Embark, disembark, deck/below-deck, crew transfer, reorder, promote
  ShipMovement.js        — Ship movement, turning, reachable tile computation
  ShipPlacement.js       — Ship creation, placement validation, placement preview
  ShipState.js           — Sinking, derelict, repair, naval state advancement
  ShipVisuals.js         — Ship sprite creation and visual updates

managers/storage/
  StorageAdapter.js      — Abstract storage interface
  WebStorageAdapter.js   — localStorage-based save/load
  ElectronStorageAdapter.js — File-based save/load via Electron IPC
  getStorageAdapter.js   — Factory: returns Electron or Web adapter based on environment

managers/ui/
  ConfirmDialogUI.js     — Reusable confirmation dialog with Yes/No
  ComingSoonPanel.js     — "Coming Soon" placeholder modal
  HeroCardPopup.js       — Hero detail card with stats, abilities, aura info
  ManageCrewPanel.js     — Ship crew management panel
  MapHighlightUI.js      — Map tile highlighting (movement, attack range)
  MapSizePanel.js        — Map size selection for new game
  MedievalUI.js          — Shared medieval-themed UI button/panel factory
  ModalHelper.js         — Reusable modal panel creation (blocker + container + destroy cleanup)
  PauseOptionsPanel.js   — Pause menu with save/load/options
  ProductionReportUI.js  — Production overview report
  ShipPlacementConfirmUI.js — Ship placement confirmation dialog
  StatBreakdown.js       — Combat stat breakdown data
  StatBreakdownPanel.js  — Visual stat breakdown panel
  StatPanelUI.js         — Unit stat display panel

electron/
  main.cjs               — Electron main process
  preload.cjs            — Electron preload script (IPC bridge for file storage)

assets/
  test-map.json          — Test map data
  tiles/                 — Tile sprite assets and blob lookup tables
    blob_lookup.js       — Bitmask → sprite frame mapping
    blob_lookup.json     — Same as JSON

documentation/           — Design docs, feature plans, implementation notes
tools/                   — Python scripts for generating tile sprite sheets
```

## Architecture & Key Patterns

### Scene Flow
MainMenuScene → (optional) IslandSelectScene → GameScene

### State Management
`GameState` is a plain object factory (`createGameState()`) holding all mutable game state. Managers receive it on construction. Scene keeps backward-compatible aliases (`scene.selectedUnit`, etc.) during ongoing migration.

### Manager Pattern
GameScene creates and owns 13+ manager instances. Each manager receives `scene` (for Phaser APIs) and operates on shared state. Naval functionality is decomposed into 7 sub-modules under `managers/naval/` with `NavalManager` as a facade.

### Modal System
`ModalHelper.createModalPanel()` handles blocker creation, depth management, and cleanup. `CONFIG.MODAL_PANEL_KEYS` is the single source of truth for which panels exist (used by InputManager for Escape and CameraManager for scroll-blocking).

### Config-Driven Design
Nearly all tunable values live in `config.js`: unit stats, terrain costs/effects, map generation parameters, economy values, AI behavior, UI layout constants, ship classes. Terrain types define movement cost, defense bonuses, damage modifiers, and healing.

### Map Generation
Procedural island generation with retry logic for naval connectivity:
1. `MapTerrainGenerator` — creates island shape with water edges, inlets, terrain distribution
2. `MapLocationPlacer` — places named forts, orc camps, ports, workshops with distance constraints
3. `MapPathingService` — generates road network via MST connecting forts, A* pathing

### Combat System
Grid-based with flanking bonuses, terrain modifiers (forest damage reduction, fort defense bonus, bridge damage bonus), siege units, and naval bombardment. Heroes provide passive aura buffs to nearby allies and have active abilities with cooldowns. Mage spell animation: red/blue team colors; undead mage uses same lightning in bright green.

### Economy
Territory-based income (forts/camps generate gold per turn), unit maintenance costs, loot from capturing tiles, construction costs. Initial army-assembly budget of 2,500g. AI follows the same economic rules.

### Movement & Pathing
- Units move step-by-step along paths (`Unit.moveAlongPath`). Reachable tiles exclude occupied tiles (no moving through or ending on another unit).
- **Path planning** for distant targets (Path Move / persistent path) ignores other units; when executing, if a step is blocked by another unit, movement stops, "Blocked" is shown, and the path is trimmed. Immediate move (click reachable tile) uses pathfinding with unit avoidance.
- **Camera:** Red/Orc turn — camera follows the active unit. Blue Path Move and Move All — camera follows the moving unit step-by-step.

### Production Restrictions
Forts: buildable land units depend on fort size (`CONFIG.PRODUCTION.FORT_SIZE_UNIT_KEYS`; 9/8-tile get full roster; smaller forts fewer types). Ports build only ships (Liburna, Galley, Trireme). Red fort auto-spawn and blue production validate against these.

### Naval System
Ships are multi-tile floating terrain (Liburna 1-tile, Galley 2-tile, Trireme 3-tile) with deck/below-deck crew slots, hull HP, bombardment range, and boarding/disembarking mechanics.

### Save System
Pluggable storage: `WebStorageAdapter` (localStorage) for browser, `ElectronStorageAdapter` (file-based via IPC) for desktop. Save data compressed with lz-string.

## Development

```bash
npm install          # Install dependencies
npm run dev          # Start Vite dev server (browser)
npm start            # Run in Electron desktop window
npm run build        # Package with electron-builder
```

## Conventions

- All game code is JavaScript ESM (import/export)
- No TypeScript, no build step for game code (Vite serves directly)
- Config values go in `config.js`, not scattered in managers
- New UI panels should use `ModalHelper.createModalPanel()` and register in `CONFIG.MODAL_PANEL_KEYS`
- Terrain bitmask computation lives in `MapManager`
- Entity classes extend `Unit` (Phaser Container subclass)
- Teams are `'blue'` (player), `'red'` (AI enemy), `'orc'` (AI enemy) — defined in `CONFIG.TEAMS`
- Active modes (attack, heal, build, etc.) defined in `CONFIG.ACTIVE_MODES`

## Known Architecture Notes

- ~18k lines of JavaScript across 57+ files
- Largest files: ProductionManager, UIManager, CombatManager, InputManager, SpawnManager; GameScene, ManageCrewPanel, PauseOptionsPanel, config, AnimationManager, Unit, MapPathingService (see documentation/js-files-line-count.md)
- UIManager refactored to delegate to managers/ui/ (MedievalUI, MapHighlightUI, ManageCrewPanel, StatPanelUI, ProductionReportUI, ShipPlacementConfirmUI)
- Managers still read/write `scene.*` properties heavily; migration to GameState is partial
- SiegeManager and EconomyManager are small stubs / WIP
- See `plan.md` and `refactor-2-25-26.md` for refactoring roadmap
- For full config schema and embedded documentation (difficulty, engineer, etc.), see `llms-full.txt`


## Technical Schema (config.js)
```javascript
export const CONFIG = {
    TILE_SIZE: 64,
    MAP_WIDTH: 100,
    MAP_HEIGHT: 100,
    // Starting army selection constraints
    STARTING_ARMY_BUDGET: 3000,
    ECONOMY: {
        INITIAL_POOL: 3000,
        SETUP_BUDGET: 2500,
        INCOME_FORT_TILE: 20,
        INCOME_ORC_TILE: 100,
        /** Orcs get this much gold per orc camp they own each turn. */
        INCOME_ORC_CAMP: 50,
        LOOT_TILE_MIN: 25,
        LOOT_TILE_MAX: 100,
        FORT_CAPTURE_BASE: 200,
        ORC_CAMP_CAPTURE_BASE: 350
    },
    BUILD_COSTS: {
        ROAD: 10,
        WORKSHOP: 150,
        CASTRA: 200
    },
    // UI layout and panel styling (single source for resize/theme)
    UI: {
        SCREEN_WIDTH: 1920,
        SCREEN_HEIGHT: 1080,
        SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH: 1920 * 0.2,
        BOTTOM_PANEL_HEIGHT: 100,
        PANEL_BG: 0x5c4033,
        PANEL_TRIM: 0xc5a059,
        PANEL_INNER_STROKE: 0x2a1a12,
        PANEL_RADIUS: 18,
        BUTTON_BG: 0x6a3f2b,
        /** Modal overlay depths for dialogs (blocker just below panel). */
        MODAL_BLOCKER_DEPTH: 6998,
        MODAL_PANEL_DEPTH: 6999
    },
    /** Panel keys for modal overlays; used by InputManager (Escape) and CameraManager (scroll). */
    MODAL_PANEL_KEYS: ['heroSelectionPanel', 'unitSelectionPanel', 'startInstructionsPanel', 'productionPanel', 'locationsPanel', 'reportPanel', 'manageCrewPanel', 'pauseMenuPanel', 'loadSlotPanel', 'difficultyPanel', 'mapSizePanel', 'waveAnnouncementPanel'],
    TEAMS: { BLUE: 'blue', RED: 'red', ORC: 'orc' },
    DIFFICULTY_LEVELS: { EASY: 'easy', NORMAL: 'normal', HARD: 'hard' },
    /** Active difficulty — set by applyDifficultyOverrides(), read by spawn/AI systems. */
    currentDifficulty: 'normal',
    ACTIVE_MODES: { ATTACK: 'attack', HEAL_UNIT: 'heal_unit', REPAIR: 'repair', BUILD_ROAD: 'build_road', BUILD_WORKSHOP: 'build_workshop', BUILD_CASTRA: 'build_castra', HERO_ABILITY: 'hero_ability' },
    AI: {
        AGGRO_RANGE: {
            RED: 15,
            ORC: 10
        },
        /** Red fort production: weight unit choice by cost so cheaper units are more likely. Weight = (maxCost - cost + 1)^exponent. 0 = uniform, 1 = linear bias, >1 = stronger bias toward cheap. */
        RED_PRODUCTION_COST_WEIGHT_EXPONENT: 1.5
    },
    TERRAIN: {
        GRASS: { key: 'tile-grass', cost: 1, name: 'Grass' },
        FOREST: { key: 'tile-forest', cost: 2, name: 'Forest', damageReduction: 0.25 },
        MOUNTAIN: { key: 'tile-mountain', cost: Infinity, name: 'Mountain' },
        FORT: { key: 'tile-fort', cost: 1, name: 'Fort', damageReduction: 0.5, defenseBonus: 3, startTurnHeal: 0.2 },
        WATER: { key: 'tile-water', cost: Infinity, name: 'Water' },
        SAND: { key: 'tile-sand', cost: 2, name: 'Sand' },
        BRIDGE: { key: 'tile-bridge', cost: 0.5, name: 'Bridge', damageBonus: 0.25 },
        ROAD: { key: 'tile-road', cost: 0.5, name: 'Road' },
        ORC_CAMP: { key: 'tile-orc-camp', cost: 1, name: 'Orc Camp' },
        PORT: { key: 'tile-port', cost: 1, name: 'Port', startTurnHeal: 0.1 },
        JETTY: { key: 'tile-jetty', cost: 0.5, name: 'Jetty' },
        SHIP_DECK: { key: 'tile-bridge', cost: 1, name: 'Ship Deck', defenseBonus: 0.1 },
        WORKSHOP: { key: 'tile-workshop', cost: 1, name: 'Workshop' },
        CASTRA: { key: 'tile-castra', cost: 1, name: 'Castra', damageReduction: 0.25, defenseBonus: 2, startTurnHeal: 0.1 }
    },
    SHIP_CLASSES: {
        LIBURNA: {
            key: 'ship-liburna',
            name: 'Liburna',
            tiles: 1,
            deckSlots: 1,
            capacity: 3,
            moves: 18,
            hullHp: 40,
            shipAtk: 8,
            shipDef: 3,
            bombardRange: 3,
            cost: 300,
            maintenance: 15
        },
        GALLEY: {
            key: 'ship-galley',
            name: 'Galley',
            tiles: 2,
            deckSlots: 2,
            capacity: 6,
            moves: 12,
            hullHp: 80,
            shipAtk: 15,
            shipDef: 6,
            bombardRange: 3,
            cost: 600,
            maintenance: 30
        },
        TRIREME: {
            key: 'ship-trireme',
            name: 'Trireme',
            tiles: 3,
            deckSlots: 3,
            capacity: 10,
            moves: 9,
            hullHp: 130,
            shipAtk: 24,
            shipDef: 10,
            bombardRange: 3,
            cost: 1000,
            maintenance: 50
        }
    },
    UNIT_CLASSES: {
        INFANTRY: { 
            key: 'unit-infantry', name: 'Infantry', range: 1, minRange: 1, moves: 5, 
            stats: { hp: 50, atk: 15, def: 5 },
            growth: { hp: 10, atk: 3, def: 2 },
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 220,
            maintenance: 7
        },
        KNIGHT: {
            key: 'unit-knight', name: 'Knight', range: 1, minRange: 1, moves: 7,
            stats: { hp: 40, atk: 20, def: 4 },
            growth: { hp: 8, atk: 4, def: 1 },
            bonusAgainst: [{ target: 'unit-archer', bonus: 5 }, { target: 'unit-healer', bonus: 5 }, { target: 'unit-mage', bonus: 5 }],
            /** Cavalry suffer 50% attack penalty when attacking targets on fort tiles. */
            attackPenaltyOnTerrain: [{ type: 'FORT', factor: 0.5 }],
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 365,
            maintenance: 12
        },
        ARCHER: { 
            key: 'unit-archer', name: 'Archer', range: 3, minRange: 2, moves: 5, 
            stats: { hp: 30, atk: 12, def: 3 },
            growth: { hp: 6, atk: 3, def: 1 },
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 200,
            maintenance: 6
        },
        MAGE: { 
            key: 'unit-mage', name: 'Mage', range: 2, minRange: 1, moves: 4, 
            stats: { hp: 25, atk: 25, def: 2 },
            growth: { hp: 5, atk: 5, def: 1 },
            attackType: 'magic',
            damageReduction: { magic: 0.5 },
            cost: 250,
            maintenance: 10
        },
        HEALER: { 
            key: 'unit-healer', name: 'Healer', range: 2, minRange: 1, moves: 5, 
            stats: { hp: 30, atk: 6, def: 3, isHealer: true },
            growth: { hp: 7, atk: 2, def: 2 },
            attackType: 'magic',
            cost: 295,
            maintenance: 8
        },
        SPEAR: {
            key: 'unit-spear', name: 'Spearman', range: 1, minRange: 1, moves: 5,
            stats: { hp: 35, atk: 12, def: 4 },
            growth: { hp: 7, atk: 3, def: 2 },
            bonusAgainst: [{ target: 'unit-knight', bonus: 10 }, { target: 'unit-orc-rider-no-bg', bonus: 10 }],
            /** Spearmen take 50% damage from cavalry (Knight, Orc Rider). */
            receiveDamageFactorFrom: [{ attackerKey: 'unit-knight', factor: 0.5 }, { attackerKey: 'unit-orc-rider-no-bg', factor: 0.5 }],
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 180,
            maintenance: 5
        },
        CATAPULT: {
            key: 'unit-catapult',
            name: 'Catapult',
            range: 4,
            minRange: 2,
            moves: 2,
            stats: { hp: 20, atk: 16, def: 0 },
            growth: { hp: 2, atk: 3, def: 1 },
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 400,
            maintenance: 20,
            canBoardShip: true,
            bonusAgainstLocation: [{ type: 'FORT', bonusPercent: 100 }, { type: 'PORT', bonusPercent: 100 }, { type: 'ORC_CAMP', bonusPercent: 100 }]
        },
        BALLISTA: {
            key: 'unit-ballista',
            name: 'Ballista',
            range: 3,
            minRange: 1,
            moves: 3,
            stats: { hp: 30, atk: 25, def: 2 },
            growth: { hp: 6, atk: 1, def: 1 },
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 300,
            maintenance: 15,
            canBoardShip: true
        },
        ENGINEER: {
            key: 'unit-engineer',
            name: 'Engineer',
            range: 1,
            minRange: 1,
            moves: 5,
            stats: { hp: 34, atk: 8, def: 3 },
            growth: { hp: 7, atk: 2, def: 1 },
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 320,
            maintenance: 10,
            engineerSkills: ['build_road', 'build_workshop', 'build_castra', 'repair']
        },
        ORC_INFANTRY: {
            key: 'unit-orc-infantry-no-bg', name: 'Orc Infantry', range: 1, minRange: 1, moves: 5,
            stats: { hp: 60, atk: 18, def: 7 },
            growth: { hp: 12, atk: 4, def: 3 },
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 150,
            maintenance: 5
        },
        ORC_MAGE: {
            key: 'unit-orc-mage-no-bg', name: 'Orc Mage', range: 2, minRange: 1, moves: 4,
            stats: { hp: 40, atk: 28, def: 3 },
            growth: { hp: 8, atk: 6, def: 1 },
            attackType: 'magic',
            damageReduction: { magic: 0.5 },
            cost: 350,
            maintenance: 10
        },
        ORC_RIDER: {
            key: 'unit-orc-rider-no-bg', name: 'Orc Rider', range: 1, minRange: 1, moves: 7,
            stats: { hp: 70, atk: 20, def: 8 },
            growth: { hp: 15, atk: 5, def: 4 },
            /** Cavalry suffer 50% attack penalty when attacking targets on fort tiles. */
            attackPenaltyOnTerrain: [{ type: 'FORT', factor: 0.5 }],
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 450,
            maintenance: 15
        },
        ORC_SPEAR: {
            key: 'unit-orc-spear-no-bg', name: 'Orc Spearman', range: 1, minRange: 1, moves: 5,
            stats: { hp: 55, atk: 15, def: 8 },
            growth: { hp: 11, atk: 3, def: 3 },
            /** Spearmen take 50% damage from cavalry (Knight, Orc Rider). */
            receiveDamageFactorFrom: [{ attackerKey: 'unit-knight', factor: 0.5 }, { attackerKey: 'unit-orc-rider-no-bg', factor: 0.5 }],
            attackType: 'physical',
            cost: 180,
            maintenance: 6
        },
        ORC_HEALER: {
            key: 'unit-orc-healer-no-bg', name: 'Orc Healer', range: 2, minRange: 1, moves: 5,
            stats: { hp: 45, atk: 12, def: 4, isHealer: true },
            growth: { hp: 9, atk: 2, def: 2 },
            attackType: 'magic',
            cost: 280,
            maintenance: 8
        }
    },
    HEROES: {
        MARCUS: {
            id: 'MARCUS',
            name: 'Marcus, the Centurion',
            portraitKey: 'hero-marcus',
            baseClass: 'INFANTRY',
            cost: 500,
            maintenance: 15,
            bonusStats: { hp: 15, atk: 5, def: 3, mov: 1, rng: 0 },
            growth: { hp: 12, atk: 4, def: 3 },
            aura: {
                range: 4,
                bonuses: { mov: 1, rng: 1, atk: 2, def: 3, hp: 2 },
                caps:    { mov: 5, rng: 5, atk: 7, def: 9, hp: 7 }
            },
            ability: {
                id: 'TESTUDO', name: 'Testudo',
                description: '+10 DEF, no crits, MOV clamped to 2 for all units in 3×3 square. Lasts 2 turns.',
                targetType: 'friendly', shape: 'square', range: 1,
                duration: 2, cooldown: 4
            }
        },
        VALERIA: {
            id: 'VALERIA',
            name: 'Valeria, the Shield Maiden',
            portraitKey: 'hero-valeria',
            baseClass: 'SPEAR',
            cost: 480,
            maintenance: 12,
            bonusStats: { hp: 10, atk: 4, def: 4, mov: 1, rng: 0 },
            growth: { hp: 10, atk: 3, def: 3 },
            aura: {
                range: 4,
                bonuses: { mov: 1, rng: 1, atk: 1, def: 2, hp: 3 },
                caps:    { mov: 5, rng: 5, atk: 5, def: 7, hp: 9 }
            },
            ability: {
                id: 'BULWARK', name: 'Bulwark',
                description: '+8 DEF to allies within 2 tiles, +12 DEF to Valeria for 2 turns.',
                targetType: 'friendly', shape: 'radius', range: 2,
                duration: 2, cooldown: 4
            }
        },
        RODERIC: {
            id: 'RODERIC',
            name: 'Roderic, the Cavalier',
            portraitKey: 'hero-roderic',
            baseClass: 'KNIGHT',
            cost: 600,
            maintenance: 18,
            bonusStats: { hp: 10, atk: 6, def: 2, mov: 1, rng: 0 },
            growth: { hp: 10, atk: 5, def: 2 },
            aura: {
                range: 4,
                bonuses: { mov: 2, rng: 1, atk: 3, def: 1, hp: 1 },
                caps:    { mov: 7, rng: 5, atk: 9, def: 5, hp: 5 }
            },
            ability: {
                id: 'LUNGE', name: 'Lunge',
                description: 'Dash to enemy within 3 tiles, strike at 2× ATK. Kill grants Canto.',
                targetType: 'enemy', shape: 'single', range: 3,
                duration: 0, cooldown: 3
            }
        },
        SYLARA: {
            id: 'SYLARA',
            name: 'Sylara, the Hawkeye',
            portraitKey: 'hero-sylara',
            baseClass: 'ARCHER',
            cost: 520,
            maintenance: 14,
            bonusStats: { hp: 8, atk: 5, def: 2, mov: 0, rng: 1 },
            growth: { hp: 8, atk: 4, def: 1 },
            aura: {
                range: 4,
                bonuses: { mov: 1, rng: 3, atk: 2, def: 1, hp: 1 },
                caps:    { mov: 5, rng: 9, atk: 7, def: 5, hp: 5 }
            },
            ability: {
                id: 'VOLLEY', name: 'Volley',
                description: 'AoE damage in 3×3 area within range 6. Enemies that move next turn take bleed damage.',
                targetType: 'enemy', shape: 'square', range: 6,
                duration: 1, cooldown: 4
            }
        },
        ALANA: {
            id: 'ALANA',
            name: 'Alana, the Battlemage',
            portraitKey: 'hero-alana',
            baseClass: 'MAGE',
            cost: 560,
            maintenance: 16,
            bonusStats: { hp: 10, atk: 6, def: 2, mov: 1, rng: 0 },
            growth: { hp: 7, atk: 6, def: 1 },
            aura: {
                range: 4,
                bonuses: { mov: 1, rng: 1, atk: 3, def: 1, hp: 2 },
                caps:    { mov: 5, rng: 5, atk: 9, def: 5, hp: 7 }
            },
            ability: {
                id: 'RUNEBLAST', name: 'Runeblast',
                description: '20 magic damage ignoring DEF (2× vs Mages). Weakens target ATK by 4 for 2 turns.',
                targetType: 'enemy', shape: 'single', range: 4,
                duration: 2, cooldown: 4
            }

            maintenance: 14,
            bonusStats: { hp: 12, atk: 3, def: 3, mov: 1, rng: 0 },

            aura: {

                bonuses: { mov: 1, rng: 1, atk: 1, def: 2, hp: 3 },

    DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS: {
        easy: {

            enemyDefMult: 1.0,

            aggroRangeOrc: 10,

            islandDefenseChance: 0.25,
            redProductionCostWeightExponent: 1.5,
            fortSpawnRateMult: 1.0,
            orcCampSpawnDelayMult: 1.0
        },

            label: 'Hard',
            description: 'Aggressive AI, elite enemies',
            enemyHpMult: 1.20,
            enemyAtkMult: 1.15,
            enemyDefMult: 1.15,
            aggroRangeRed: 20,
            aggroRangeOrc: 14,
            cityDefenseChance: 0.40,
            islandDefenseChance: 0.35,
            redProductionCostWeightExponent: 0.5,
            fortSpawnRateMult: 0.75,
            orcCampSpawnDelayMult: 0.75
        }
    },
    MAP_GENERATION: {
        // Base island shape
        BASE_LAND_THRESHOLD: 35,

        // Terrain features
        MOUNTAIN_RIDGE_COUNT: 15,
        MOUNTAIN_RIDGE_MAX_LENGTH: 20,
        FOREST_CLUSTER_COUNT: 40,
        FOREST_CLUSTER_RADIUS: 5,
        LAKE_COUNT: 6,
        RIVER_COUNT: 5,

        // Naval/coast shaping
        EDGE_INLET_COUNT: 6,
        CROSS_CHANNEL_MIN_COUNT: 1,
        CROSS_CHANNEL_MAX_COUNT: 3,
        CROSS_CHANNEL_WIDTH_MIN: 3,
        CROSS_CHANNEL_WIDTH_MAX: 5,
        PASSAGEWAY_MIN_COUNT: 5,
        PASSAGEWAY_MAX_COUNT: 8,
        PASSAGEWAY_MIN_LENGTH: 20,
        PASSAGEWAY_MAX_LENGTH: 49,
        PASSAGEWAY_WIDTH_MIN: 2,
        PASSAGEWAY_WIDTH_MAX: 5,

        WATER_NAVIGATION_PROTECTION_PASSES: 2,

        ROAD_MP_COST_WATER_OR_MOUNTAIN: 3,

        BRIDGE_WATER_WIDTH_PENALTY: 1.1,

        ROAD_BRIDGE_REFINEMENT_PASSES: 2,

        // Post-generation validation/retry
        MIN_WATER_RATIO: 0.55,
        MIN_COAST_TO_COAST_WATER_PATHS: 1,
        NAVAL_VALIDATION_MAX_RETRIES: 4,

        // Locations (capture: each tile has HP; must be reduced to 0 by attacks before capture)
        LOCATION_TILE_HP: 100,
        PORT_TILE_HP: 50,
        WORKSHOP_TILE_HP: 50,
        CASTRA_TILE_HP: 50,
        FORT_COUNT: 12,
        FORT_MIN_TILES: 4,
        FORT_MAX_TILES: 9,
        FORT_MIN_DISTANCE: 10,
        ORC_CAMP_MIN_DISTANCE: 12,
        ORC_CAMP_MIN_COUNT: 5,
        ORC_CAMP_MAX_COUNT: 10,

        PORT_COUNT_MAX: 6,
        PORT_MIN_DISTANCE: 12,
        PORT_MAX_DISTANCE_FROM_FORT: 5,
        PORT_SPAWN_RATE: 5
    },

    // Production restrictions: fort size -> land units; ports: ships only
    PRODUCTION: {
        FORT_SIZE_UNIT_KEYS: {
            9: ['INFANTRY', 'KNIGHT', 'ARCHER', 'MAGE', 'HEALER', 'SPEAR', 'CATAPULT', 'BALLISTA', 'ENGINEER'],
            8: ['INFANTRY', 'KNIGHT', 'ARCHER', 'MAGE', 'HEALER', 'SPEAR', 'CATAPULT', 'BALLISTA', 'ENGINEER'],
            7: ['INFANTRY', 'ARCHER', 'MAGE', 'HEALER', 'SPEAR', 'BALLISTA', 'ENGINEER'],
            6: ['INFANTRY', 'ARCHER', 'HEALER', 'SPEAR', 'ENGINEER'],
            5: ['INFANTRY', 'ARCHER', 'SPEAR'],
            4: ['INFANTRY', 'SPEAR'],
            3: ['INFANTRY'],
            2: ['INFANTRY'],
            1: ['INFANTRY']
        },
        PORT_SHIP_KEYS: ['LIBURNA', 'GALLEY', 'TRIREME'],
        PORT_SHIP_BUILD_TURNS: { LIBURNA: 2, GALLEY: 4, TRIREME: 6 },
        WORKSHOP_UNIT_KEYS: ['BALLISTA', 'CATAPULT'],
        WORKSHOP_BUILD_TURNS: { BALLISTA: 2, CATAPULT: 4 }
    }
};

/**
 * Apply difficulty overrides to CONFIG. Call BEFORE applyMapSizeOverrides().
 * @param {'easy'|'normal'|'hard'} level
 */
export function applyDifficultyOverrides(level) {
    const settings = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[level];
    if (!settings) return;
    CONFIG.currentDifficulty = level;

    CONFIG.AI.AGGRO_RANGE.RED = settings.aggroRangeRed;
    CONFIG.AI.AGGRO_RANGE.ORC = settings.aggroRangeOrc;
    CONFIG.AI.RED_PRODUCTION_COST_WEIGHT_EXPONENT = settings.redProductionCostWeightExponent;
}

/**
 * Mutate CONFIG.MAP_WIDTH, MAP_HEIGHT, and all MAP_GENERATION / AI / CAMERA
 * values to match different map sizes. Call BEFORE MapManager.generateMap().
 * Base reference is 100×100. Counts scale by area ratio; distances by side ratio.
 */
export function applyMapSizeOverrides(width, height) {
    CONFIG.MAP_WIDTH = width;
    CONFIG.MAP_HEIGHT = height;
    const A = (width * height) / (100 * 100);   // area ratio
    const S = width / 100;                        // side ratio
    const gen = CONFIG.MAP_GENERATION;

    // Island shape
    gen.BASE_LAND_THRESHOLD          = Math.round(35 * S);

    // Terrain features — counts scale by area, lengths/radii by side
    gen.MOUNTAIN_RIDGE_COUNT         = Math.max(2,  Math.round(15 * A));
    gen.MOUNTAIN_RIDGE_MAX_LENGTH    = Math.max(5,  Math.round(20 * S));
    gen.FOREST_CLUSTER_COUNT         = Math.max(4,  Math.round(40 * A));
    gen.FOREST_CLUSTER_RADIUS        = Math.max(2,  Math.round(5  * S));
    gen.LAKE_COUNT                   = Math.max(1,  Math.round(6  * A));
    gen.RIVER_COUNT                  = Math.max(1,  Math.round(5  * A));

    // Naval / coast shaping
    gen.EDGE_INLET_COUNT             = Math.max(1,  Math.round(6  * S));
    gen.CROSS_CHANNEL_MIN_COUNT      = Math.max(0,  Math.round(1  * A));
    gen.CROSS_CHANNEL_MAX_COUNT      = Math.max(1,  Math.round(3  * A));
    gen.CROSS_CHANNEL_WIDTH_MIN      = Math.max(2,  Math.round(3  * S));
    gen.CROSS_CHANNEL_WIDTH_MAX      = Math.max(2,  Math.round(5  * S));
    gen.PASSAGEWAY_MIN_COUNT         = Math.max(1,  Math.round(5  * A));
    gen.PASSAGEWAY_MAX_COUNT         = Math.max(2,  Math.round(8  * A));
    gen.PASSAGEWAY_MIN_LENGTH        = Math.max(5,  Math.round(20 * S));
    gen.PASSAGEWAY_MAX_LENGTH        = Math.max(10, Math.round(49 * S));
    gen.PASSAGEWAY_WIDTH_MIN         = Math.max(1,  Math.round(2  * S));
    gen.PASSAGEWAY_WIDTH_MAX         = Math.max(2,  Math.round(5  * S));
    gen.MAX_BRIDGE_LENGTH            = Math.max(3,  Math.round(10 * S));

    // Locations — counts by area, distances by side
    gen.FORT_COUNT                   = Math.max(2,  Math.round(12 * A));
    gen.FORT_MIN_DISTANCE            = Math.max(4,  Math.round(10 * S));
    gen.ORC_CAMP_MIN_DISTANCE        = Math.max(4,  Math.round(12 * S));
    gen.ORC_CAMP_MIN_COUNT           = Math.max(1,  Math.round(5  * A));
    gen.ORC_CAMP_MAX_COUNT           = Math.max(2,  Math.round(10 * A));
    gen.PORT_COUNT_MIN               = Math.max(1,  Math.round(4  * A));
    gen.PORT_COUNT_MAX               = Math.max(1,  Math.round(6  * A));
    gen.PORT_MIN_DISTANCE            = Math.max(4,  Math.round(12 * S));
    gen.PORT_MAX_DISTANCE_FROM_FORT  = Math.max(3,  Math.round(5  * S));

    // Micro maps: more land, smaller forts, tighter distances, guaranteed locations
    if (width <= 25) {
        gen.FORT_MAX_TILES = Math.min(gen.FORT_MAX_TILES, 5);
        gen.FORT_MIN_TILES = Math.min(gen.FORT_MIN_TILES, 3);
        gen.FORT_COUNT = Math.max(2, gen.FORT_COUNT);
        gen.FORT_MIN_DISTANCE = 3;
        gen.ORC_CAMP_MIN_COUNT = Math.max(1, gen.ORC_CAMP_MIN_COUNT);
        gen.ORC_CAMP_MAX_COUNT = Math.max(2, gen.ORC_CAMP_MAX_COUNT);
        gen.ORC_CAMP_MIN_DISTANCE = 3;
        gen.MIN_WATER_RATIO = 0.35;             // more land so forts/camps can fit
        gen.BASE_LAND_THRESHOLD = 13;           // ~30% more land (11 * sqrt(1.3) ≈ 12.5)
        gen.CROSS_CHANNEL_MIN_COUNT = 0;         // skip channels that carve away land
        gen.CROSS_CHANNEL_MAX_COUNT = 0;
        gen.PASSAGEWAY_MIN_COUNT = 1;
        gen.PASSAGEWAY_MAX_COUNT = 2;
    }

    // AI aggro range
    CONFIG.AI.AGGRO_RANGE.RED  = Math.max(5, Math.round(15 * S));
    CONFIG.AI.AGGRO_RANGE.ORC  = Math.max(4, Math.round(10 * S));

    // Camera — smaller maps need to zoom out less
    CONFIG.CAMERA.MIN_ZOOM = Math.max(0.1, 0.155 * S);

    // Ship start-placement min enemy distance
    CONFIG.NAVAL.START_PLACEMENT_MIN_DISTANCE_FROM_ENEMY_SHIP = Math.max(3, Math.round(6 * S));
}
```

## Documentation: difficulty-impl-plan.md
# Difficulty System — Implementation Plan (Alternative B)

Step-by-step implementation for the **AI Behavior & Stat Scaling** difficulty system.
Each step is self-contained and testable. Steps 1–4 are the core; steps 5–7 are polish.

---

## Step 1: Config — Define difficulty constants and `applyDifficultyOverrides()`

**File:** `config.js`

### 1a. Add difficulty enum and current-difficulty field

Add near the top of CONFIG, alongside `TEAMS`:

```js
DIFFICULTY_LEVELS: { EASY: 'easy', NORMAL: 'normal', HARD: 'hard' },
/** Active difficulty — set by applyDifficultyOverrides(), read by spawn/AI systems. */
currentDifficulty: 'normal',
```

### 1b. Add `DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS` data table

Add after `MAP_SIZES` (or at the end of CONFIG). Every tunable lives here so balancing is pure data:

```js
DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS: {
    easy: {
        label: 'Easy',
        description: 'Forgiving enemies, shorter aggro range',
        // Enemy stat multipliers (applied to non-blue units at spawn)
        enemyHpMult:  0.80,
        enemyAtkMult: 0.85,
        enemyDefMult: 0.85,
        // AI behavior
        aggroRangeRed: 10,
        aggroRangeOrc: 7,
        cityDefenseChance: 0.15,
        // Production
        redProductionCostWeightExponent: 2.5,   // heavy bias toward cheap units
        fortSpawnRateMult: 1.5,                  // slower enemy production (higher = more turns)
        orcCampSpawnDelayMult: 1.4,              // orc camps also slower
    },
    normal: {
        label: 'Normal',
        description: 'The intended experience',
        enemyHpMult:  1.0,
        enemyAtkMult: 1.0,
        enemyDefMult: 1.0,
        aggroRangeRed: 15,
        aggroRangeOrc: 10,
        cityDefenseChance: 0.25,
        redProductionCostWeightExponent: 1.5,
        fortSpawnRateMult: 1.0,
        orcCampSpawnDelayMult: 1.0,
    },
    hard: {
        label: 'Hard',
        description: 'Aggressive AI, elite enemies',
        enemyHpMult:  1.20,
        enemyAtkMult: 1.15,
        enemyDefMult: 1.15,
        aggroRangeRed: 20,
        aggroRangeOrc: 14,
        cityDefenseChance: 0.40,
        redProductionCostWeightExponent: 0.5,   // bias toward elite units
        fortSpawnRateMult: 0.75,                // faster enemy production
        orcCampSpawnDelayMult: 0.75,
    }
},
```

### 1c. Add `applyDifficultyOverrides(level)` export

Place right above the existing `applyMapSizeOverrides()` function. The menu calls this **before** `applyMapSizeOverrides()` so map-size scaling can further adjust aggro ranges on top of difficulty.

```js
/**
 * Apply difficulty overrides to CONFIG. Call BEFORE applyMapSizeOverrides().
 * @param {'easy'|'normal'|'hard'} level
 */
export function applyDifficultyOverrides(level) {
    const settings = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[level];
    if (!settings) return;
    CONFIG.currentDifficulty = level;

    // AI behavior overrides (base values — applyMapSizeOverrides will scale further)
    CONFIG.AI.AGGRO_RANGE.RED = settings.aggroRangeRed;
    CONFIG.AI.AGGRO_RANGE.ORC = settings.aggroRangeOrc;
    CONFIG.AI.RED_PRODUCTION_COST_WEIGHT_EXPONENT = settings.redProductionCostWeightExponent;
}
```

> **Why not put stat multipliers here?** Stat multipliers are read at spawn-time from `CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[CONFIG.currentDifficulty]`, not baked into CONFIG. This keeps the settings table as the single source and avoids stale values after save/load.

### Testing checkpoint

- `CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS.easy.enemyHpMult === 0.8` — purely data, no runtime impact yet.
- Existing game behavior is unchanged (`currentDifficulty` defaults to `'normal'`, multipliers are all 1.0).

---

## Step 2: Difficulty Selection UI — `DifficultyPanel.js`

**New file:** `managers/ui/DifficultyPanel.js`

### Design

Three horizontal cards (one row), modeled on `MapSizePanel.js`:
- **EASY** — shield icon, green accent, "Forgiving enemies, shorter aggro range"
- **NORMAL** — sword icon, gold accent, "The intended experience"
- **HARD** — skull icon, red accent, "Aggressive AI, elite enemies"

Each card shows the difficulty label, description from `DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS`, and a visual accent color.

### Scaffold

```js
import Phaser from 'phaser';
import { CONFIG } from '../../config.js';
import { createModalPanel } from './ModalHelper.js';

const { SCREEN_WIDTH, SCREEN_HEIGHT, PANEL_BG, PANEL_TRIM,
        MODAL_BLOCKER_DEPTH, MODAL_PANEL_DEPTH } = CONFIG.UI;

/**
 * Show the difficulty selection panel.
 * @param {Phaser.Scene} scene
 * @param {(level: string) => void} onSelect — called with 'easy'|'normal'|'hard'
 * @param {() => void} onBack
 */
export function showDifficultyPanel(scene, onSelect, onBack) {
    const panelKey = 'difficultyPanel';
    if (scene[panelKey]) return;

    const PW = 820;
    const PH = 420;

    const { panel } = createModalPanel(scene, panelKey, {
        blockerAlpha: 0.62,
        blockerDepth: MODAL_BLOCKER_DEPTH,
        panelDepth: MODAL_PANEL_DEPTH,
        panelX: SCREEN_WIDTH / 2,
        panelY: SCREEN_HEIGHT / 2,
        onBeforeDestroy: () => { scene.isModalActive = false; }
    });
    scene.isModalActive = true;

    // Panel background
    const bg = scene.add.graphics();
    bg.fillStyle(PANEL_BG, 1);
    bg.fillRoundedRect(-PW / 2, -PH / 2, PW, PH, 18);
    bg.lineStyle(4, PANEL_TRIM, 1);
    bg.strokeRoundedRect(-PW / 2, -PH / 2, PW, PH, 18);
    panel.add(bg);

    // Title
    panel.add(scene.add.text(0, -PH / 2 + 36, 'CHOOSE DIFFICULTY', {
        fontSize: '36px', fill: '#ffe9b5', fontStyle: 'bold',
        stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 6
    }).setOrigin(0.5));

    // Difficulty cards — three across
    const levels  = ['easy', 'normal', 'hard'];
    const accents = { easy: 0x4caf50, normal: 0xc5a059, hard: 0xd32f2f };
    const cardW = 220, cardH = 200, gap = 30;
    const totalW = levels.length * cardW + (levels.length - 1) * gap;
    const startX = -totalW / 2 + cardW / 2;

    levels.forEach((lvl, i) => {
        const settings = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[lvl];
        const cx = startX + i * (cardW + gap);
        const cy = 20;   // vertically centered in panel
        const accent = accents[lvl];

        const card = scene.add.container(cx, cy);

        const cg = scene.add.graphics();
        const drawCard = (hover) => {
            cg.clear();
            cg.fillStyle(hover ? 0x7a5d45 : 0x4a3728, 1);
            cg.fillRoundedRect(-cardW / 2, -cardH / 2, cardW, cardH, 12);
            cg.lineStyle(3, hover ? 0xffd700 : accent, 1);
            cg.strokeRoundedRect(-cardW / 2, -cardH / 2, cardW, cardH, 12);
        };
        drawCard(false);

        cg.setInteractive(
            new Phaser.Geom.Rectangle(-cardW / 2, -cardH / 2, cardW, cardH),
            Phaser.Geom.Rectangle.Contains
        );
        cg.on('pointerover', () => drawCard(true));
        cg.on('pointerout',  () => drawCard(false));
        cg.on('pointerdown', () => {
            panel.destroy();
            onSelect(lvl);
        });
        card.add(cg);

        // Label
        card.add(scene.add.text(0, -50, settings.label.toUpperCase(), {
            fontSize: '28px', fill: '#e8d5a3', fontStyle: 'bold',
            stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 4
        }).setOrigin(0.5));

        // Description (word-wrapped)
        card.add(scene.add.text(0, 10, settings.description, {
            fontSize: '16px', fill: '#8a7a5a', fontStyle: 'italic',
            stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 2,
            wordWrap: { width: cardW - 30 }, align: 'center'
        }).setOrigin(0.5));

        panel.add(card);
    });

    // BACK button
    const backBtn = _makeBtn(scene, 0, PH / 2 - 50, 'BACK', () => {
        panel.destroy();
        if (onBack) onBack();
    });
    panel.add(backBtn);
}

// Reuse the same _makeBtn helper from MapSizePanel
function _makeBtn(scene, x, y, label, onClick) { /* identical to MapSizePanel._makeBtn */ }
```

### Modal key registration

Add `'difficultyPanel'` to `CONFIG.MODAL_PANEL_KEYS` so Escape and camera-scroll logic handle it.

---

## Step 3: Wire the Menu Flow — Difficulty → Map Size → GameScene

**File:** `scenes/MainMenuScene.js`

### Current flow

```
_onSkirmish()
  └─ showMapSizePanel → onSelect(size) → scene.start('GameScene', { mapWidth, mapHeight })
```

### New flow

```
_onSkirmish()
  └─ showDifficultyPanel → onSelect(level)
       └─ applyDifficultyOverrides(level)
            └─ showMapSizePanel → onSelect(size)
                 └─ applyMapSizeOverrides(size.width, size.height)
                      └─ scene.start('GameScene', { mapWidth, mapHeight, difficulty: level })
```

### Code change

```js
import { showDifficultyPanel } from '../managers/ui/DifficultyPanel.js';
import { applyDifficultyOverrides } from '../config.js';  // add to existing import

_onSkirmish() {
    showDifficultyPanel(this, (level) => {
        applyDifficultyOverrides(level);
        showMapSizePanel(this, (size) => {
            applyMapSizeOverrides(size.width, size.height);
            this.scene.start('GameScene', {
                mapWidth: size.width,
                mapHeight: size.height,
                difficulty: level
            });
        }, () => {
            // BACK from map-size → re-show difficulty
            this._onSkirmish();
        });
    }, () => {
        // BACK from difficulty → return to menu
    });
}
```

### Testing checkpoint

- Clicking SKIRMISH shows three difficulty cards.
- Picking one shows the map size panel.
- BACK from map-size returns to difficulty.
- BACK from difficulty returns to menu.
- The game starts and `CONFIG.currentDifficulty` matches the chosen level.

---

## Step 4: Apply Stat Multipliers at Unit Spawn

This is the core gameplay hook. Every non-blue unit gets its HP/ATK/DEF multiplied.

### 4a. `entities/Unit.js` — constructor stat scaling

After the base stats are assigned (lines 34–37), add:

```js
// Difficulty: scale stats for non-player units
if (team !== CONFIG.TEAMS.BLUE) {
    const diff = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[CONFIG.currentDifficulty];
    if (diff) {
        this.hp     = Math.round(this.hp  * diff.enemyHpMult);
        this.maxHp  = this.hp;
        this.atk    = Math.round(this.atk * diff.enemyAtkMult);
        this.def    = Math.round(this.def * diff.enemyDefMult);
    }
}
```

This covers:
- Initial RED/ORC units spawned by `SpawnManager.spawnInitialUnits()`
- RED fort production units via `ProductionManager`
- ORC camp production units via `ProductionManager`
- Any future non-blue unit creation — it's in the base class.

### 4b. `entities/Hero.js` — hero stat scaling (enemy heroes, if any)

Currently only blue heroes exist, but for future-proofing add the same block **after** the bonus stats are applied (after line ~41):

```js
// Difficulty: scale stats for non-player heroes
if (team !== CONFIG.TEAMS.BLUE) {
    const diff = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[CONFIG.currentDifficulty];
    if (diff) {
        this.hp     = Math.round(this.hp  * diff.enemyHpMult);
        this.maxHp  = this.hp;
        this.atk    = Math.round(this.atk * diff.enemyAtkMult);
        this.def    = Math.round(this.def * diff.enemyDefMult);
    }
}
```

### 4c. Level-up growth scaling

When units level up, growth stats (hp, atk, def per level) should also respect difficulty.  In `Unit.js`, locate the `levelUp()` method and wrap the growth application:

```js
// In the level-up growth block:
let growthHp  = this.classData.growth.hp;
let growthAtk = this.classData.growth.atk;
let growthDef = this.classData.growth.def;

if (this.team !== CONFIG.TEAMS.BLUE) {
    const diff = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[CONFIG.currentDifficulty];
    if (diff) {
        growthHp  = Math.round(growthHp  * diff.enemyHpMult);
        growthAtk = Math.round(growthAtk * diff.enemyAtkMult);
        growthDef = Math.round(growthDef * diff.enemyDefMult);
    }
}
```

### Testing checkpoint

- Start a game on Easy → inspect an orc unit → HP should be ~80% of config value.
- Start a game on Hard → same unit → HP should be ~120%.
- Normal mode → no change vs. current behavior.

---

## Step 5: AI Behavior Tuning

### 5a. City defense roll — `managers/AIManager.js`

Currently hardcoded at line 75:

```js
if (Math.random() >= 0.25) return;
```

Change to:

```js
const defenseChance = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[CONFIG.currentDifficulty]?.cityDefenseChance ?? 0.25;
if (Math.random() >= defenseChance) return;
```

### 5b. Fort spawn rate — `managers/ProductionManager.js`

The RED fort spawn rate is calculated at line 155:

```js
loc.production.spawnRate = Math.max(1, CONFIG.MAP_GENERATION.FORT_MAX_TILES - (loc.tiles ? loc.tiles.length : 1) + 3);
```

Multiply by the difficulty factor:

```js
const baseRate = CONFIG.MAP_GENERATION.FORT_MAX_TILES - (loc.tiles ? loc.tiles.length : 1) + 3;
const spawnMult = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[CONFIG.currentDifficulty]?.fortSpawnRateMult ?? 1;
loc.production.spawnRate = Math.max(1, Math.round(baseRate * spawnMult));
```

On Easy (mult 1.5) a 9-tile fort goes from spawnRate 3 → 5 turns. On Hard (mult 0.75) it goes 3 → 2 turns.

### 5c. Orc camp spawn delay

Orc camps randomize a respawn timer at line 133:

```js
loc.production.turnsLeft = Math.floor(Math.random() * 13) + 12;
```

Apply the orc camp multiplier:

```js
const baseTurns = Math.floor(Math.random() * 13) + 12;
const orcMult = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[CONFIG.currentDifficulty]?.orcCampSpawnDelayMult ?? 1;
loc.production.turnsLeft = Math.max(1, Math.round(baseTurns * orcMult));
```

### Testing checkpoint

- On Easy: AI units mostly stick near their forts; production is visibly slower.
- On Hard: AI is aggressive at long range; forts pump out units noticeably faster.

---

## Step 6: Persist Difficulty in Save/Load

### 6a. `GameState.js` — add field

```js
return {
    // ... existing fields ...
    difficulty: CONFIG.currentDifficulty ?? 'normal',   // ← add
};
```

### 6b. `scenes/GameScene.js` — read from `init(data)`

```js
init(data) {
    this.saveData = data?.saveData ?? null;

    // Apply difficulty overrides (new game or load)
    const difficulty = this.saveData?.difficulty ?? data?.difficulty ?? 'normal';
    applyDifficultyOverrides(difficulty);                    // ← add

    if (!this.saveData && data?.mapWidth && data?.mapHeight) {
        applyMapSizeOverrides(data.mapWidth, data.mapHeight);
    }
    if (this.saveData) {
        const w = this.saveData.map?.width ?? 100;
        const h = this.saveData.map?.height ?? 100;
        applyMapSizeOverrides(w, h);
    }
}
```

Import `applyDifficultyOverrides` alongside the existing `applyMapSizeOverrides` import.

### 6c. `managers/SaveManager.js` — serialize

In `serialize()`, add to the returned object:

```js
difficulty: CONFIG.currentDifficulty ?? 'normal',
```

### 6d. `managers/SaveManager.js` — deserialize

In `deserialize()`, near the top (before unit reconstruction):

```js
if (data.difficulty) {
    applyDifficultyOverrides(data.difficulty);
}
```

Import `applyDifficultyOverrides` from `'../config.js'`.

### 6e. Save migration

Saves without `difficulty` default to `'normal'` via the `?? 'normal'` fallback — no migration function needed. Existing saves load seamlessly.

### Testing checkpoint

- Save on Hard, reload → enemy stats are still scaled to Hard.
- Load a pre-difficulty save → defaults to Normal, no errors.

---

## Step 7: Polish & QA

### 7a. Tooltip / HUD indicator

Show the current difficulty on the bottom HUD bar (next to turn/gold display) so the player always knows.

In `UIManager.js`, when building the bottom panel status text, append:

```js
const diffLabel = CONFIG.DIFFICULTY_SETTINGS[CONFIG.currentDifficulty]?.label ?? 'Normal';
// Include in status text, e.g.: "Turn 5 · Normal · 1,200 Gold"
```

### 7b. Healer priority tweak (optional, stretch)

In `AIManager`, when the AI has a healer and a friendly unit is below 50% HP, increase heal priority on Hard:

```js
// In target scoring for healers:
if (CONFIG.currentDifficulty === 'hard' && attacker.classData.stats?.isHealer) {
    // prefer healing over attacking
}
```

This is a future enhancement — skip for V1.

### 7c. Balance testing matrix

| Difficulty | Map Size | Verify |
|---|---|---|
| Easy | Large | Player snowballs by turn 15; enemies feel weak |
| Easy | Micro | Quick win, relaxed |
| Normal | Large | Current balance — no regression |
| Normal | Micro | Current balance — no regression |
| Hard | Large | Resource pressure; need tactical play |
| Hard | Micro | Very tight; every unit matters |

---

| # | File | Type | Lines Changed (est.) |
|---|---|---|---|

| 10 | `managers/SaveManager.js` | Edit | +6 (serialize + deserialize) |
