Add a simple colored overlay graphic per tile that has active status effects. Use a semi-transparent sprite or rectangle drawn on the map layer. Update each tick:
- DESECRATED: dark purple semi-transparent overlay (`0x660066`, alpha 0.3)
- CORPSE: gray skull icon overlay

**Verification:** Kill a unit near a Necromancer, confirm corpse appears. Wait for Necromancer turn, confirm Zombie is raised. Walk Engineer over Desecrated tile, confirm it's cleansed.

---

## Phase 7: Sanctified Ground, Mist Form & Wave Activation

### Step 7.1 — Sanctified ground (fort aura)
Create `_getSanctifiedTileSet()` in `TurnManager.js`:
```js
_getSanctifiedTileSet() {
    const set = new Set();
    const range = CONFIG.UNDEAD_ESCALATION.SANCTIFIED_RANGE;
    for (const loc of this.scene.mapManager.locations) {
        if (loc.type !== 'FORT' || loc.owner !== CONFIG.TEAMS.BLUE) continue;
        for (const tileKey of Object.keys(loc.tileOwners)) {
            const [fx, fy] = tileKey.split(',').map(Number);
            for (let dx = -range; dx <= range; dx++) {
                for (let dy = -range; dy <= range; dy++) {
                    if (Math.abs(dx) + Math.abs(dy) <= range) {
                        set.add(`${fx + dx},${fy + dy}`);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return set;
}
```

Add `processSanctifiedDamage()` — called during Red turn-start:
```js
processSanctifiedDamage() {
    const sanctified = this._getSanctifiedTileSet();
    const dmg = CONFIG.UNDEAD_ESCALATION.SANCTIFIED_DAMAGE;
    for (const unit of this.scene.gameState.units) {
        if (!unit.undead || unit.isDying) continue;
        if (sanctified.has(`${unit.gridX},${unit.gridY}`)) {
            unit.hp = Math.max(1, unit.hp - dmg);
            unit.updateHpBar();
            addUnitMessage(unit, 'damage', `Sanctified ground burns! (-${dmg} HP)`, '#ffd700');
        }
    }
}
```

### Step 7.2 — Mist Form movement processing
Create `processMistForm()` in `TurnManager.js`, called during Red turn-start:
```js
processMistForm() {
    const cfg = CONFIG.UNDEAD_ESCALATION;
    const vampires = this.scene.gameState.units.filter(
        u => u.isHero && u.vampireHero && u.mistForm && !u.isDying
    );
    for (const vamp of vampires) {
        if (!vamp.mistFormTarget) {
            // No Red fort to flee to → permanent death
            this._permanentKillVampire(vamp);
            continue;
        }
        // Check if target fort is still Red-owned
        const targetFort = this.scene.mapManager.locations.find(loc =>
            loc.type === 'FORT' && loc.owner === CONFIG.TEAMS.RED
            && Object.keys(loc.tileOwners).includes(`${vamp.mistFormTarget.x},${vamp.mistFormTarget.y}`)
        );
        if (!targetFort) {
            this._permanentKillVampire(vamp);
            continue;
        }
        // Move up to 2 tiles toward target
        const path = this.scene.mapPathingService.findPath(
            vamp.gridX, vamp.gridY,
            vamp.mistFormTarget.x, vamp.mistFormTarget.y
        );
        const steps = Math.min(cfg.MIST_FORM_MOVE_TILES, path.length - 1);
        if (steps > 0) {
            const dest = path[steps];
            // Check if tile is blocked by a Blue unit
            const blocker = this.scene.gameState.units.find(
                u => u.gridX === dest.x && u.gridY === dest.y && u.team === CONFIG.TEAMS.BLUE
            );
            if (blocker) {
                this._permanentKillVampire(vamp);
                continue;
            }
            // Move
            vamp.gridX = dest.x;
            vamp.gridY = dest.y;
            vamp.setPosition((dest.x + 0.5) * CONFIG.TILE_SIZE, (dest.y + 0.5) * CONFIG.TILE_SIZE);
        }
        // Check if arrived at fort
        if (vamp.gridX === vamp.mistFormTarget.x && vamp.gridY === vamp.mistFormTarget.y) {
            // Queue regeneration
            this._queueVampireRegen(vamp, targetFort);
        }
    }
}
```

```js
_permanentKillVampire(vamp) {
    vamp.mistForm = false;
    addUnitMessage(vamp, 'death', `${vamp.heroData.name} is permanently destroyed!`, '#ff0000');
    // Trigger Frenzy on all leashed undead (handled next tick by processFrenzyChecks)
    vamp.die(); // calls super.die() since mistForm is now false
}
```

```js
_queueVampireRegen(vamp, fort) {
    // Remove from board, add to regen queue
    vamp.setVisible(false);
    vamp.mistForm = false;

    if (!this._vampireRegenQueue) this._vampireRegenQueue = [];
    this._vampireRegenQueue.push({
        heroKey: vamp.heroKey,
        turnsRemaining: CONFIG.UNDEAD_ESCALATION.MIST_FORM_REGEN_TURNS,
        fortLocationIndex: this.scene.mapManager.locations.indexOf(fort)
    });

    // Temporarily remove from units array
    this.scene.gameState.units = this.scene.gameState.units.filter(u => u !== vamp);
    this.scene.heroManager.activeHeroes = this.scene.heroManager.activeHeroes.filter(h => h !== vamp);
    vamp.destroy();
}
```

Add regen tick to Red turn-start (in `endTurn()`):
```js
_tickVampireRegen() {
    if (!this._vampireRegenQueue) return;
    for (let i = this._vampireRegenQueue.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
        const entry = this._vampireRegenQueue[i];
        entry.turnsRemaining--;
        if (entry.turnsRemaining <= 0) {
            // Respawn at fort
            const fort = this.scene.mapManager.locations[entry.fortLocationIndex];
            if (fort && fort.owner === CONFIG.TEAMS.RED) {
                const tileKey = Object.keys(fort.tileOwners)[0];
                const [sx, sy] = tileKey.split(',').map(Number);
                const hero = new Hero(this.scene, sx, sy, entry.heroKey, CONFIG.TEAMS.RED);
                hero.hp = Math.round(hero.maxHp * CONFIG.UNDEAD_ESCALATION.MIST_FORM_REGEN_HP_PERCENT);
                this.scene.gameState.units.push(hero);
                this.scene.heroManager.activeHeroes.push(hero);
                this.scene.add.existing(hero);
                addUnitMessage(hero, 'status', `${hero.heroData.name} has regenerated!`, '#9966ff');
            }
            this._vampireRegenQueue.splice(i, 1);
        }
    }
}
```

### Step 7.3 — Wave activation system (UPDATED — Random Hero Selection)

**Current implementation:** Hero spawning no longer uses fixed wave triggers (fort captures, unit counts, etc.). Instead, it uses an **interval-based schedule** scaled by map size with **random hero selection** from `UNDEAD_HERO_POOL`.

#### Spawn schedule (`_getEnemyHeroSpawnSchedule()`)
| Map Width | First Spawn | Interval | Max Heroes |
|-----------|-------------|----------|------------|
| ≤25 (micro) | Turn 4 | 4 turns | 1 |
| ≤50 (small) | Turn 6 | 6 turns | 2 |
| ≤75 (medium) | Turn 8 | 8 turns | 3 |
| 100 (large) | Turn 10 | 10 turns | 4 |

#### Key methods in `TurnManager.js`:
- `_isEnemyHeroSpawnTurn()` — checks if current turn matches the schedule
- `_pickRandomUndeadHero()` — picks randomly from `UNDEAD_HERO_POOL`, excluding already-spawned heroes (tracked in `_undeadWaveState.alreadySpawnedUndeadHeroes`)
- `_showWaveAnnouncementAndSpawn(waveNum, heroKey, cfg)` — looks up the hero-specific introduction from `cfg.HERO_INTRODUCTIONS[heroKey]` (title, message, whatFollows) and shows it via `WaveAnnouncementPanel`. The panel pauses the enemy turn until the player closes it, then calls `_spawnVampireHero(heroKey)`.
- `_spawnVampireHero(heroKey)` — spawns the hero at the largest Red fort

```js
async _showWaveAnnouncementAndSpawn(waveNum, heroKey, cfg) {
    const heroData = CONFIG.HEROES?.[heroKey];
    const heroName = heroData?.name ?? heroKey;
    const intro = cfg.HERO_INTRODUCTIONS?.[heroKey];
    const hero = {
        portraitKey: heroData?.portraitKey ?? `hero-${heroKey.toLowerCase()}`,
        name: heroName
    };
    await WaveAnnouncementPanel.showWaveAnnouncement(this.scene, {
        title: intro?.title ?? 'A DARK PRESENCE AWAKENS',
        hero,
        message: intro?.message ?? `${heroName} has risen from the grave.`,
        whatFollows: intro?.whatFollows ?? [],
        onClose: () => this._spawnVampireHero(heroKey)
    });
}
```

### Step 7.4 — Track Vampire Hero kills
In `_permanentKillVampire()`, add:
```js
if (this._undeadWaveState) this._undeadWaveState.vampireHeroKills++;
```

### Step 7.5 — Wire all into `endTurn()`
In the Red turn-start section of `TurnManager.endTurn()`, the new calls go in this order:
```js
// --- Undead Legion processing (Red turn-start) ---
this.checkUndeadWaveActivation();
this.processMistForm();
this._tickVampireRegen();
this.processCorruptionCounters();
this.processFrenzyChecks();
this.processSanctifiedDamage();
this.tickTileStatusEffects();
```

**Verification:** Play through a full game. At each scheduled spawn turn, a random hero is selected from the pool and announced via a unique introduction panel. Confirm no repeats until the pool is exhausted, and spawning stops at the map-size max.

---

## Phase 8: Combat Modifiers, Aura Filter & Save/Load

### Step 8.1 — Engineer 2× damage vs undead
In `CombatManager.finishAction()`, after calculating `atkPower` and before applying defense, add:
```js
if (attacker.classKey === 'ENGINEER' && target.undead) {
    atkPower *= CONFIG.COMBAT.ENGINEER_UNDEAD_DAMAGE_MULT;
}
```

### Step 8.2 — Vampire aura filter in `HeroManager.getAuraBonus()`
In the existing loop that checks each active hero, add a guard:
```js
// Skip vampire hero aura for non-undead units
if (hero.vampireHero && !unit.undead) continue;
```

### Step 8.3 — Vampire Hero AI ability usage in `AIManager.processTeamTurn()`
After existing healer/siege/attacker logic, add hero-ability AI for Vampire Heroes:
```js
if (unit.isHero && unit.vampireHero && unit.abilityCooldown === 0) {
    // Attempt to use ability before normal attack
    const abilityUsed = this.scene.heroManager.tryAIAbility(unit);
    if (abilityUsed) continue;
}
```

Add `tryAIAbility(hero)` to `HeroManager.js`:
```js
tryAIAbility(hero) {
    const ability = hero.heroData.ability;
    if (hero.abilityCooldown > 0) return false;

    switch (ability.id) {
        case 'PLAGUE_CLOUD': {
            // Find cluster of ≥2 enemies within ability range
            const targets = this.getEnemyClusters(hero, ability.range, 'square', 1);
            if (targets) { this.executeAbility(hero, targets); return true; }
            return false;
        }
        case 'TERROR_CHARGE': {
            // Find high-value enemy within 4 tiles
            const enemy = this._findBestAbilityTarget(hero, ability.range);
            if (enemy) { this.executeAbility(hero, { x: enemy.gridX, y: enemy.gridY }); return true; }
            return false;
        }
        case 'SOUL_SIPHON': {
            // Use when Livia or adjacent undead are damaged
            if (hero.hp < hero.maxHp * 0.7) {
                const enemy = this._findBestAbilityTarget(hero, ability.range);
                if (enemy) { this.executeAbility(hero, { x: enemy.gridX, y: enemy.gridY }); return true; }
            }
            return false;
        }
        case 'BONE_BARRAGE': {
            // Find cluster of enemies at range 6
            const targets = this.getEnemyClusters(hero, ability.range, 'square', 1);
            if (targets) { this.executeAbility(hero, targets); return true; }
            return false;
        }
        default: return false;
    }
}
```

### Step 8.4 — Save/Load support in `SaveManager.js`

**serializeUnit()** — add these fields:
```js
undead: unit.undead,
corruptionCounter: unit.corruptionCounter,
isNecromancer: unit.isNecromancer,
mistForm: unit.mistForm || false,
mistFormTarget: unit.mistFormTarget || null,
vampireHero: unit.vampireHero || false
```

**deserialize()** — after restoring existing unit fields, add:
```js
u.undead = saved.undead || false;
u.corruptionCounter = saved.corruptionCounter || 0;
u.isNecromancer = saved.isNecromancer || false;
if (u.isHero) {
    u.vampireHero = saved.vampireHero || false;
    u.mistForm = saved.mistForm || false;
    u.mistFormTarget = saved.mistFormTarget || null;
}
if (u.undead) u.sprite.setTint(0x66ff66);
if (u.corruptionCounter > 0) u.updateCorruptionOverlay();
```

**Serialize tile status effects** — add to the save payload:
```js
tileStatusEffects: [...gameState.tileStatusEffects.entries()]
```

**Deserialize tile status effects:**
```js
gameState.tileStatusEffects = new Map(saved.tileStatusEffects || []);
```

**Serialize wave state & regen queue:**
```js
undeadWaveState: turnManager._undeadWaveState || null,
vampireRegenQueue: turnManager._vampireRegenQueue || []
```

Deserialize those and assign back to `turnManager._undeadWaveState` and `turnManager._vampireRegenQueue`.

**Verification:** Save mid-game with active undead. Load. Confirm all undead state is preserved: tints, corruption counters, mist form, wave progress, tile effects.

---

## Testing Checklist

| # | Test Case | Expected Result |
|---|-----------|-----------------|
| 1 | Reach spawn turn (map-size dependent) | Random hero spawns at largest Red fort with unique introduction panel |
| 2 | Each hero spawns | Hero-specific title, message, and "What follows" shown in announcement panel |
| 3 | Kill a Vampire Hero | Enters Mist Form, moves toward Red fort |
| 4 | Block mist form path | Hero permanently dies, undead enter Frenzy |
| 5 | Frenzied unit near Red unit | Attacks Red unit (friendly fire) |
| 6 | Red infantry near Vampire Hero for 3 turns | Corruption counter fills, transforms to undead |
| 7 | Blue unit on Sanctified ground | No corruption, undead take 10 dmg/turn |
| 8 | Engineer walks over Desecrated tile | Tile cleansed automatically |
| 9 | Engineer attacks undead | 2× damage |
| 10 | Necromancer near a kill | Corpse placed, Necromancer raises Zombie |
| 11 | Bone Barrage on Blue cluster | Immediate damage + Desecrated tiles for 2 turns |
| 12 | Soul Siphon with adjacent undead | Target damaged, Livia + adjacent undead healed |
| 13 | Save with active undead, reload | All state preserved (tints, counters, wave progress) |
| 14 | All Vampire Heroes killed | No more corruption, remaining undead all frenzied |
| 15 | Max heroes reached | No more spawns after reaching map-size cap |

---

## File Change Summary

| File | Type | Changes |
|------|------|---------|
| `config.js` | Modify | Add `UNDEAD_TRANSFORMS`, 4 Vampire Hero entries, `UNDEAD_ESCALATION`, `ENGINEER_UNDEAD_DAMAGE_MULT` |
| `entities/Unit.js` | Modify | Add `undead`, `corruptionCounter`, `isNecromancer` fields + `updateCorruptionOverlay()` |
| `entities/Hero.js` | Modify | Add `vampireHero`, `mistForm`, `mistFormTarget` fields + Mist Form die() override + `_findNearestRedFort()` |
| `managers/TurnManager.js` | Modify | Add 7 new methods: corruption, frenzy, sanctified, mist form, regen, tile effects, wave activation |
| `managers/AIManager.js` | Modify | Add Frenzy AI override, Necromancer raise logic, Vampire Hero ability AI |
| `managers/CombatManager.js` | Modify | Add Engineer 2× undead damage, corpse placement on kill, FEARED counter-attack block |
| `managers/HeroManager.js` | Modify | Add 4 ability cases, vampire aura filter, `tryAIAbility()`, DISEASED/FEARED tick logic |
| `managers/AnimationManager.js` | Modify | Add 4 ability animation methods + corruption tint + mist form visual |
| `managers/SaveManager.js` | Modify | Serialize/deserialize undead fields, tile status effects, wave state, regen queue |
| `GameState.js` | Modify | Add `tileStatusEffects: new Map()` |


## Documentation: unit-message-impl-plan.md
# Unit Message Log — Step-by-Step Implementation Plan

> **Purpose**: This document provides exact, copy-paste-ready instructions for implementing the Unit Message Log feature. Each step specifies the file, line numbers, exact code to find, and exact code to insert or replace. Execute steps in order.

---

## Prerequisites

Read the design document at `documentation/unit-message-mgmt.md` for context on the feature goals.

**Key constants referenced throughout:**
- `CONFIG.UI.SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH` = `384` (1920 × 0.2)
- `CONFIG.UI.SCREEN_HEIGHT` = `1080`
- `CONFIG.UI.COLORS.PANEL_BG` = `0x5c4033`
- `CONFIG.UI.COLORS.PANEL_TRIM` = `0xc5a059`
- `CONFIG.UI.COLORS.PANEL_INNER_STROKE` = `0x2a1a12`

---

## Step 1: Create `managers/ui/UnitMessageLog.js`

Create a new file at `managers/ui/UnitMessageLog.js` with the following contents. This is the only new file needed.

```js
import { CONFIG } from '../../config.js';

// ─── Constants ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
const PANEL_BG     = 0x0f0b07;
const PANEL_TRIM   = CONFIG.UI.PANEL_TRIM;
const PANEL_STROKE = CONFIG.UI.PANEL_INNER_STROKE;
const ICON_SIZE    = 36;
const ICON_X       = CONFIG.UI.SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16;
const ICON_Y       = 16;
const PANEL_X      = ICON_X;
const PANEL_Y      = ICON_Y + ICON_SIZE + 8;   // 60
const PANEL_W      = 340;
const PANEL_H      = 520;
const CONTENT_TOP  = 54;
const LINE_H       = 20;
const DEPTH_ICON   = 3150;
const DEPTH_PANEL  = 3160;

const CATEGORY_COLORS = {
    damage:  '#ff0000',
    heal:    '#00ff00',
    exp:     '#88ccff',
    levelup: '#ffd700',
    ability: '#ffd700',
    counter: '#ff8844',
    buff:    '#88ddff',
    status:  '#ff8844',
    gold:    '#ffd700',
    blocked: '#ffcc00',
    canto:   '#ffff00',
    misc:    '#cccccc',
};

// ─── Module State ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// We track the icon container, panel container, and which unit's log is shown.
// These live on the scene as scene._msgLogIcon, scene._msgLogPanel.

// ─── Helper: push a message onto a unit's log ──────────────────────────────

/**
 * Add a message entry to a unit's message log.
 * Call this alongside every floating-text creation.
 *
 * @param {import('../../entities/Unit.js').Unit} unit
 * @param {string} category - One of the CATEGORY_COLORS keys.
 * @param {string} text     - Human-readable message.
 * @param {string} [color]  - Hex color override (defaults to category color).
 */
export function addUnitMessage(unit, category, text, color) {
    if (!unit || !unit.messageLog) return;
    const g = unit.scene?.gameState;
    unit.messageLog.push({
        turn: g?.turnCount ?? 0,
        category,
        text,
        color: color || CATEGORY_COLORS[category] || '#cccccc',
    });
}

// ─── Icon ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Show the small message-log toggle icon.  Call when a unit is selected.
 * If already showing for the same unit, do nothing.
 * If showing for a different unit, rebuild.
 */
export function showIcon(scene) {
    if (scene._msgLogIcon) return;             // already visible

    const c = scene.add.container(ICON_X, ICON_Y).setScrollFactor(0).setDepth(DEPTH_ICON);
    scene.cameras.main.ignore(c);

    // Background
    const bg = scene.add.graphics();
    bg.fillStyle(CONFIG.UI.PANEL_BG, 1);
    bg.fillRoundedRect(0, 0, ICON_SIZE, ICON_SIZE, 8);
    bg.lineStyle(2, PANEL_TRIM, 1);
    bg.strokeRoundedRect(0, 0, ICON_SIZE, ICON_SIZE, 8);
    // Three horizontal lines glyph
    bg.lineStyle(2, 0xffd700, 0.9);
    bg.beginPath();
    bg.moveTo(8, 10);  bg.lineTo(28, 10);
    bg.moveTo(8, 18);  bg.lineTo(28, 18);
    bg.moveTo(8, 26);  bg.lineTo(22, 26);
    bg.strokePath();
    c.add(bg);

    // Hover overlay (drawn on top, alpha 0 normally)
    const hoverOverlay = scene.add.graphics();
    hoverOverlay.fillStyle(0xffffff, 0.15);
    hoverOverlay.fillRoundedRect(0, 0, ICON_SIZE, ICON_SIZE, 8);
    hoverOverlay.setAlpha(0);
    c.add(hoverOverlay);

    // Interactive hit zone
    const hitZone = scene.add.zone(ICON_SIZE / 2, ICON_SIZE / 2, ICON_SIZE, ICON_SIZE)
        .setInteractive({ useHandCursor: true });
    hitZone.on('pointerover', () => hoverOverlay.setAlpha(1));
    hitZone.on('pointerout',  () => hoverOverlay.setAlpha(0));
    hitZone.on('pointerdown', () => {
        const g = scene.gameState ?? scene;
        if (g.selectedUnit) togglePanel(scene, g.selectedUnit);
    });
    c.add(hitZone);

    scene._msgLogIcon = c;
}

/**
 * Hide and destroy the icon (and the panel if open).
 */
export function hideIcon(scene) {
    hidePanel(scene);
    if (scene._msgLogIcon) {
        scene._msgLogIcon.destroy();
        scene._msgLogIcon = null;
    }
}

// ─── Panel ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Build and show the scrollable message log panel for a unit.
 */
export function showPanel(scene, unit) {
    if (scene._msgLogPanel) hidePanel(scene);   // destroy stale
    if (!unit || !unit.messageLog) return;

    const panel = scene.add.container(PANEL_X, PANEL_Y).setScrollFactor(0).setDepth(DEPTH_PANEL).setAlpha(0);
    scene.cameras.main.ignore(panel);

    // ── Background ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    const bg = scene.add.graphics();
    bg.fillStyle(PANEL_BG, 0.95);
    bg.fillRoundedRect(0, 0, PANEL_W, PANEL_H, 14);
    bg.lineStyle(4, PANEL_TRIM, 1);
    bg.strokeRoundedRect(0, 0, PANEL_W, PANEL_H, 14);
    bg.lineStyle(2, PANEL_STROKE, 0.8);
    bg.strokeRoundedRect(8, 8, PANEL_W - 16, PANEL_H - 16, 10);
    panel.add(bg);

    // ── Title ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    const unitName = unit.classData?.name || 'Unit';
    const title = scene.add.text(PANEL_W / 2, 20, `${unitName} — Log`, {
        fontSize: '20px', fill: '#ffd700', fontStyle: 'bold',
        stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 3
    }).setOrigin(0.5, 0);
    panel.add(title);

    // ── Separator line ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    const sep = scene.add.graphics();
    sep.lineStyle(1, 0xffd700, 0.5);
    sep.beginPath();
    sep.moveTo(20, 48); sep.lineTo(PANEL_W - 20, 48);
    sep.strokePath();
    panel.add(sep);

    // ── Content container (scrollable) ──────────────────────────────────
    const contentContainer = scene.add.container(0, 0);
    panel.add(contentContainer);

    const entries = [...unit.messageLog].reverse();  // newest first
    let yOffset = CONTENT_TOP;
    let lastTurn = -1;

    if (entries.length === 0) {
        const empty = scene.add.text(PANEL_W / 2, PANEL_H / 2, 'No events recorded.', {
            fontSize: '16px', fill: '#888888', fontStyle: 'italic',
            stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 1
        }).setOrigin(0.5);
        contentContainer.add(empty);
    } else {
        for (const entry of entries) {
            if (entry.turn !== lastTurn) {
                if (lastTurn !== -1) yOffset += 6;  // extra gap between turn groups
                const turnHeader = scene.add.text(20, yOffset, `— Turn ${entry.turn} —`, {
                    fontSize: '14px', fill: '#ffd700', fontStyle: 'bold',
                    stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 2
                });
                contentContainer.add(turnHeader);
                yOffset += LINE_H;
                lastTurn = entry.turn;
            }
            const msg = scene.add.text(28, yOffset, entry.text, {
                fontSize: '15px', fill: entry.color || '#cccccc',
                stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 1,
                wordWrap: { width: PANEL_W - 60 }
            });
            contentContainer.add(msg);
            yOffset += Math.max(LINE_H, msg.height + 4);
        }
    }

    const totalContentHeight = yOffset;
    const visibleHeight = PANEL_H - CONTENT_TOP - 12;

    // ── Geometry mask for scrolling ─────────────────────────────────────
    const maskGraphics = scene.make.graphics({ x: 0, y: 0, add: false });
    maskGraphics.fillStyle(0xffffff);
    maskGraphics.fillRect(PANEL_X + 12, PANEL_Y + CONTENT_TOP, PANEL_W - 24, visibleHeight);
    const mask = maskGraphics.createGeometryMask();
    contentContainer.setMask(mask);

    // ── Scroll state ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    let scrollOffset = 0;
    const maxScroll = Math.max(0, totalContentHeight - visibleHeight);

    // Scroll indicator (small gold arrow at bottom)
    let scrollIndicator = null;
    if (maxScroll > 0) {
        scrollIndicator = scene.add.text(PANEL_W - 28, PANEL_H - 20, '▼', {
            fontSize: '14px', fill: '#ffd700', stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 1
        }).setOrigin(0.5);
        panel.add(scrollIndicator);
    }

    const updateScroll = (newOffset) => {
        scrollOffset = Phaser.Math.Clamp(newOffset, -maxScroll, 0);
        contentContainer.y = scrollOffset;
        if (scrollIndicator) {
            scrollIndicator.setAlpha(scrollOffset > -maxScroll ? 1 : 0.3);
        }
    };

    // Wheel handler — scoped to panel bounds (screen coordinates)
    const wheelHandler = (pointer, _go, _dx, deltaY) => {
        const lx = pointer.x - PANEL_X;
        const ly = pointer.y - PANEL_Y;
        if (lx < 0 || lx > PANEL_W || ly < 0 || ly > PANEL_H) return;
        updateScroll(scrollOffset - Math.sign(deltaY) * LINE_H * 2);
    };
    scene.input.on('wheel', wheelHandler);

    // Store cleanup data on panel so hidePanel can remove listeners
    panel._msgLogCleanup = () => {
        scene.input.off('wheel', wheelHandler);
        maskGraphics.destroy();
    };

    scene._msgLogPanel = panel;

    // ── Animate in ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    panel.y = PANEL_Y - 10;
    scene.tweens.add({
        targets: panel,
        alpha: 1,
        y: PANEL_Y,
        duration: 150,
        ease: 'Sine.easeOut'
    });
}

/**
 * Hide and destroy the message log panel.
 */
export function hidePanel(scene) {
    const panel = scene._msgLogPanel;
    if (!panel) return;
    if (panel._msgLogCleanup) panel._msgLogCleanup();
    scene.tweens.add({
        targets: panel,
        alpha: 0,
        duration: 100,
        onComplete: () => {
            panel.destroy();
        }
    });
    scene._msgLogPanel = null;
}

/**
 * Toggle panel open/closed for the given unit.
 */
export function togglePanel(scene, unit) {
    if (scene._msgLogPanel) {
        hidePanel(scene);
    } else {
        showPanel(scene, unit);
    }
}

/**
 * Refresh: call after unit selection changes.
 * Shows icon when a unit is selected, hides everything when deselected.
 */
export function refresh(scene) {
    const g = scene.gameState ?? scene;
    if (g.selectedUnit && g.selectedUnit.messageLog) {
        // If panel was open for a different unit, close it
        if (scene._msgLogPanel) hidePanel(scene);
        showIcon(scene);
    } else {
        hideIcon(scene);
    }
}
```

---

## Step 2: Add `messageLog` to Unit Constructor

**File:** `entities/Unit.js`  
**Line ~50** — Insert a new property after `this.actionsDisabled = false;` and before the `// Persistent movement path` comment.

**Find this code:**
```js
        this.isDefending = false;
        this.actionsDisabled = false;

        // Persistent movement path (multi-turn waypoints)
```

**Replace with:**
```js
        this.isDefending = false;
        this.actionsDisabled = false;

        // Message log for unit event history (displayed in the message log panel)
        this.messageLog = [];

        // Persistent movement path (multi-turn waypoints)
```

---

## Step 3: Instrument `Unit.takeDamage()`

**File:** `entities/Unit.js`  
**Line ~1** — Add the import at the top of the file.

**Find this code:**
```js
import Phaser from 'phaser';
import { CONFIG } from '../config.js';
import { audioManager } from '../AudioManager.js';
```

**Replace with:**
```js
import Phaser from 'phaser';
import { CONFIG } from '../config.js';
import { audioManager } from '../AudioManager.js';
import { addUnitMessage } from '../managers/ui/UnitMessageLog.js';
```

**Line ~484** — After `audioManager.playHit();` and before `// Floating Damage text`, add the message:

**Find this code:**
```js
        this.updateHpBar();
        audioManager.playHit();

        // Floating Damage text
        const dmgText = this.scene.add.text(this.x, this.y - 10, `-${finalAmount}`, {
```

**Replace with:**
```js
        this.updateHpBar();
        audioManager.playHit();

        // Log damage to message log
        addUnitMessage(this, 'damage', `Took ${finalAmount} damage`, color);

        // Floating Damage text
        const dmgText = this.scene.add.text(this.x, this.y - 10, `-${finalAmount}`, {
```

---

## Step 4: Instrument `Unit.heal()`

**File:** `entities/Unit.js`  
**Line ~514** — After `audioManager.playHeal();` and before `// Floating Heal text`:

**Find this code:**
```js
        this.updateHpBar();
        audioManager.playHeal();

        // Floating Heal text
        const healText = this.scene.add.text(this.x, this.y - 10, `+${amount}`, {
```

**Replace with:**
```js
        this.updateHpBar();
        audioManager.playHeal();

        // Log heal to message log
        addUnitMessage(this, 'heal', `Healed ${amount} HP`, color);

        // Floating Heal text
        const healText = this.scene.add.text(this.x, this.y - 10, `+${amount}`, {
```

---

**Find this code:**
    gainExp(amount) {
        this.exp += amount;
        
        // Floating EXP text
```

**Replace with:**

    gainExp(amount) {
        this.exp += amount;

        // Log EXP gain to message log
        addUnitMessage(this, 'exp', `+${amount} EXP`, '#88ccff');
        
        // Floating EXP text
```

## Step 6: Instrument `Unit.levelUp()`

**File:** `entities/Unit.js`  
**Line ~427** — After `audioManager.playLevelUp();`:

**Find this code:**
```js
        audioManager.playLevelUp();

        // Floating Level Up text
        const lvText = this.scene.add.text(this.x, this.y - 40, 'LEVEL UP!', {
            fontSize: '28px',
            fill: '#ffff00',
```

**Replace with:**
```js
        audioManager.playLevelUp();

        // Log level-up to message log
        addUnitMessage(this, 'levelup', `Leveled up to Lv ${this.level}! (HP+${growth.hp} ATK+${growth.atk} DEF+${growth.def})`, '#ffd700');

        // Floating Level Up text
        const lvText = this.scene.add.text(this.x, this.y - 40, 'LEVEL UP!', {
            fontSize: '28px',
            fill: '#ffff00',
```

---

## Step 7: Instrument `Unit.resetTurn()` Fort Healing

**File:** `entities/Unit.js`  
**Line ~604** — After the fort heal call, add a message. The `heal()` call already logs via Step 4, but add a more descriptive fort-specific message:

**Find this code:**
```js
            if (healAmount > 0 && this.hp < this.maxHp) {
                this.heal(healAmount);
                // Visual cue for terrain healing
```

**Replace with:**
```js
            if (healAmount > 0 && this.hp < this.maxHp) {
                this.heal(healAmount);
                addUnitMessage(this, 'heal', `Fort healed ${healAmount} HP at start of turn`, '#00ff00');
                // Visual cue for terrain healing
```

> Note: This will produce two heal messages (one from `heal()` and one here). If you prefer only one, remove the generic one inside `heal()` and always add contextual messages at the call sites. For V1, having both is acceptable — the generic `heal()` message shows up from all heal sources and the fort one adds context.

**Alternative (recommended):** Instead of double-logging, remove the `addUnitMessage` from inside `heal()` (Step 4) and only log at call sites with more context. This means:
- `heal()` itself does NOT log.
- Fort heal logs `"Fort healed X HP"`.
- Combat heal logs `"Healed X HP"` (at the caller in CombatManager).
- Blessing logs `"Blessing healed X HP"` (at the caller in HeroManager).

**If you choose this alternative**, skip the `addUnitMessage` line in Step 4 and instead add messages at each `heal()` call site. For V1, the simpler approach (keeping Step 4 as-is, skipping the duplicate fort message here) is fine.

---

## Step 9: Instrument `CombatManager.finishAction()` — Attacker Messages

```

**Replace with:**
```js
import { CONFIG } from '../config.js';

import { addUnitMessage } from './ui/UnitMessageLog.js';
```

### 9a: Log attacker's damage dealt (main attack)

**Line ~808** — After the `takeDamage` calls on the defender, add a message to the attacker. Find the block where `target.takeDamage` is called:

**Find this code:**
```js
                const isKill = target.hp <= effectiveDamage;
                if (isCatapultVsUnitOnFort) {
                    target.takeDamage(damageToUnit, textColor || '#ff0000', { skipTerrainReduction: true });
                } else {
                    target.takeDamage(effectiveDamage, textColor || '#ff0000');
                }

                // Location tile HP: when attacking a unit on an enemy-held fort/port/camp tile, reduce that tile's HP (jetty is non-capturable).
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                const isKill = target.hp <= effectiveDamage;
                if (isCatapultVsUnitOnFort) {
                    target.takeDamage(damageToUnit, textColor || '#ff0000', { skipTerrainReduction: true });
                } else {
                    target.takeDamage(effectiveDamage, textColor || '#ff0000');
                }

                // Log attack to message logs
                const targetName = target.classData?.name || 'enemy';
                const attackerName = attacker.classData?.name || 'unit';
                const dmgDealt = isCatapultVsUnitOnFort ? damageToUnit : effectiveDamage;
                addUnitMessage(attacker, 'damage', `Dealt ${dmgDealt} damage to ${targetName}`, '#ff0000');
                if (isKill) {
                    addUnitMessage(attacker, 'misc', `Defeated ${targetName}`, '#ffd700');
                }

                // Location tile HP: when attacking a unit on an enemy-held fort/port/camp tile, reduce that tile's HP (jetty is non-capturable).
```

### 9b: Log BLOODRAGE activation

**Find this code (in the `doEndOfCombat` closure):**

                                    fontSize: '24px',
                                    fill: '#ff0000',
```

**Add before that line:**

```

So the replacement is:

```

```

                            attacker.takeDamage(selfDmg, '#880000');
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                        } else if (eff.failSelfDamagePercent > 0 && attacker.hp > 0) {

                            addUnitMessage(attacker, 'damage', `Bloodrage backfire: took ${selfDmg} self-damage`, '#880000');

```

### 9e: Log COUNTER attack (on the attacker who receives counter-damage)

```js

So the replacement is:

                    const counterText = scene.add.text(attacker.x, attacker.y - 40, 'COUNTER!', {
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                    const counterEffectiveDamage = Math.max(1, Math.floor(counterFinalDamage * (1 - counterReduction) * (1 + counterBonus) * (CONFIG.COMBAT.COUNTER_ATTACK_DAMAGE_FACTOR ?? 0.5) * flankedCounterFactor));

                    addUnitMessage(attacker, 'counter', `Counter-attacked for ${counterEffectiveDamage} damage by ${target.classData?.name || 'enemy'}`, '#ff8844');
                    addUnitMessage(target, 'counter', `Counter-attacked ${attacker.classData?.name || 'enemy'} for ${counterEffectiveDamage} damage`, '#ff8844');
                    const counterText = scene.add.text(attacker.x, attacker.y - 40, 'COUNTER!', {
```

                fontSize: '22px',
                fill: '#ff3333',
```

**Add before:**
```js
            addUnitMessage(attacker, 'damage', `Dealt ${damage} damage to ship hull`, '#ff3333');
```

So:

**Find:**
```js
            ship.hullHp = Math.max(0, (ship.hullHp || 0) - damage);
            const mid = ship.tiles[Math.floor(ship.tiles.length / 2)];
            const worldX = (mid.x + 0.5) * CONFIG.TILE_SIZE;
            const worldY = (mid.y + 0.5) * CONFIG.TILE_SIZE - 20;
            const dmgText = scene.add.text(worldX, worldY, `-${damage}`, {
```

**Replace with:**
```js
            ship.hullHp = Math.max(0, (ship.hullHp || 0) - damage);
            addUnitMessage(attacker, 'damage', `Dealt ${damage} damage to ship hull`, '#ff3333');
            const mid = ship.tiles[Math.floor(ship.tiles.length / 2)];
            const worldX = (mid.x + 0.5) * CONFIG.TILE_SIZE;
            const worldY = (mid.y + 0.5) * CONFIG.TILE_SIZE - 20;
            const dmgText = scene.add.text(worldX, worldY, `-${damage}`, {
```

```js
    _showAbilityText(unit, text, color) {

```

**Replace with:**
```js

        addUnitMessage(unit, 'ability', `${text.replace('!', '')} activated!`, color);
        const scene = this.scene;
        const abilityText = scene.add.text(unit.x, unit.y - 40, text, {
```

This single change instruments ALL 10+ ability callers (TESTUDO, BULWARK, LUNGE, VOLLEY, RUNEBLAST, BLESSING, FORTIFY, BLOODRAGE, MAELSTROM, CANTO) because they all go through `_showAbilityText`.

## Step 11: Instrument Ability Target Effects in HeroManager

For abilities that affect OTHER units (not the hero), add messages to those targets. This provides context like "Received Testudo buff" on the buffed unit's log.

### 11a: TESTUDO — buff recipients

**Find (around line 298):**
```js
                for (const u of targets) {
                    this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                        type: 'TESTUDO',
                        turnsRemaining: ability.duration + 1, // +1 so it lasts through the duration
                        effect: { defBonus: 10, movOverride: 2, noCrit: true }
                    });
                }
                this._showAbilityText(hero, 'TESTUDO!', '#ffd700');
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                for (const u of targets) {
                    this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                        type: 'TESTUDO',
                        turnsRemaining: ability.duration + 1, // +1 so it lasts through the duration
                        effect: { defBonus: 10, movOverride: 2, noCrit: true }
                    });
                    if (u !== hero) addUnitMessage(u, 'buff', 'Testudo: +10 DEF, MOV set to 2', '#ffd700');
                }
                this._showAbilityText(hero, 'TESTUDO!', '#ffd700');
```

### 11b: BULWARK — buff recipients

**Find (around line 312):**
```js
                for (const u of targets) {
                    const defAmount = (u === hero) ? 12 : 8;
                    this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                        type: 'BULWARK',
                        turnsRemaining: ability.duration + 1,
                        effect: { defBonus: defAmount }
                    });
                }
                this._showAbilityText(hero, 'BULWARK!', '#c0c0c0');
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                for (const u of targets) {
                    const defAmount = (u === hero) ? 12 : 8;
                    this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                        type: 'BULWARK',
                        turnsRemaining: ability.duration + 1,
                        effect: { defBonus: defAmount }
                    });
                    if (u !== hero) addUnitMessage(u, 'buff', `Bulwark: +${defAmount} DEF`, '#c0c0c0');
                }
                this._showAbilityText(hero, 'BULWARK!', '#c0c0c0');
```

### 11c: VOLLEY — damage + bleed on enemies

**Find (around line 374):**
```js
                for (const u of enemies) {
                    u.takeDamage(dmg, '#ff8800');
                    // Apply bleed status
                    this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                        type: 'VOLLEY_BLEED',
                        turnsRemaining: 2, // ticks next turn
                        effect: { damageOnMove: 10 }
                    });
                }
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                for (const u of enemies) {
                    u.takeDamage(dmg, '#ff8800');
                    addUnitMessage(u, 'status', 'Volley: bleeding (10 damage on move)', '#ff8800');
                    // Apply bleed status
                    this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                        type: 'VOLLEY_BLEED',
                        turnsRemaining: 2, // ticks next turn
                        effect: { damageOnMove: 10 }
                    });
                }
```

### 11d: RUNEBLAST — damage + weaken on target

**Find (around line 400):**
```js
                target.takeDamage(dmg, '#aa00ff', { skipTerrainReduction: true });

                // Apply Weakened debuff
                this.applyStatusEffect(target, {
                    type: 'RUNEBLAST_WEAKEN',
                    turnsRemaining: ability.duration + 1,
                    effect: { atkReduction: 4 }
                });
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                target.takeDamage(dmg, '#aa00ff', { skipTerrainReduction: true });
                addUnitMessage(target, 'status', `Runeblast: weakened (-4 ATK)`, '#aa00ff');

                // Apply Weakened debuff
                this.applyStatusEffect(target, {
                    type: 'RUNEBLAST_WEAKEN',
                    turnsRemaining: ability.duration + 1,
                    effect: { atkReduction: 4 }
                });
```

### 11e: BLESSING — heal + buff on target

**Find (around line 421):**
```js
                if (healAmount > 0) target.heal(healAmount, '#ffd700');

                // Apply +15 DEF buff
                this.applyStatusEffect(target, {
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                if (healAmount > 0) target.heal(healAmount, '#ffd700');
                addUnitMessage(target, 'buff', `Blessing: +15 DEF, healed ${healAmount} HP`, '#ffd700');

                // Apply +15 DEF buff
                this.applyStatusEffect(target, {
```

### 11f: FORTIFY — buff on units at location

**Find (around line 453):**
```js
                    for (const u of unitsOnTile) {
                        this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                            type: 'FORTIFY',
                            turnsRemaining: ability.duration + 1,
                            effect: { atkBonus: 4, defBonus: 4 }
                        });
                    }
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                    for (const u of unitsOnTile) {
                        this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                            type: 'FORTIFY',
                            turnsRemaining: ability.duration + 1,
                            effect: { atkBonus: 4, defBonus: 4 }
                        });
                        addUnitMessage(u, 'buff', 'Fortify: +4 ATK, +4 DEF', '#88aaff');
                    }
```

### 11g: MAELSTROM — damage + freeze on enemies

**Find (around line 495):**
```js
                    u.takeDamage(dmg, '#00aaff', { skipTerrainReduction: true });

                    // Apply Frozen status
                    this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                        type: 'MAELSTROM_FREEZE',
                        turnsRemaining: 2,
                        effect: { movOverride: 0 }
                    });
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                    u.takeDamage(dmg, '#00aaff', { skipTerrainReduction: true });
                    addUnitMessage(u, 'status', 'Maelstrom: frozen (MOV 0)', '#00aaff');

                    // Apply Frozen status
                    this.applyStatusEffect(u, {
                        type: 'MAELSTROM_FREEZE',
                        turnsRemaining: 2,
                        effect: { movOverride: 0 }
                    });
```

## Step 14: Wire Icon Show/Hide into UIManager

### 14a: Call `refresh()` from `refreshAfterAction()`

**File:** `managers/UIManager.js`  
**Line ~1489** — Add the UnitMessageLog refresh at the end of `refreshAfterAction`:

**Find:**
```js
    refreshAfterAction(message) {
        const scene = this.scene;
        this.updateReachableTiles();
        if (message != null && scene.uiText) scene.uiText.setText(message);
        this.updateNextUnitButtonVisibility();
        if (scene.combatManager && scene.combatManager.checkCombatOpportunity) {
            scene.combatManager.checkCombatOpportunity();
        }
    }
```

**Replace with:**
```js
    refreshAfterAction(message) {
        const scene = this.scene;
        this.updateReachableTiles();
        if (message != null && scene.uiText) scene.uiText.setText(message);
        this.updateNextUnitButtonVisibility();
        if (scene.combatManager && scene.combatManager.checkCombatOpportunity) {
            scene.combatManager.checkCombatOpportunity();
        }
        // Show/hide unit message log icon based on selection
        UnitMessageLog.refresh(scene);
    }
```

---

            return;
        }
        if (scene.navalManager?.pendingStartPlacement) {
```

**Replace with:**
```js
            return;
        }
        // Close unit message log panel if open

            UnitMessageLog.hidePanel(scene);
            return;
        }
        if (scene.navalManager?.pendingStartPlacement) {
```

## Step 16: Add Right-Click Dismissal

**File:** `managers/InputManager.js`

        const scene = this.scene;
        scene.uiManager.hidePopupMenu();

        if (scene.navalManager?.pendingBombard) {
```

**Replace with:**
```js
    handleTileRightClick(x, y, pointer) {
        const scene = this.scene;
        scene.uiManager.hidePopupMenu();
        // Close unit message log panel on right-click
        if (scene._msgLogPanel) {
            UnitMessageLog.hidePanel(scene);
        }

        if (scene.navalManager?.pendingBombard) {
```

---

## Step 17: Hide Icon/Panel on Enemy Turn Start

**File:** `managers/TurnManager.js`  

The import was added in Step 12. Now add a call when the enemy turn starts. 

**Find (around line 20–25):**
```js
            g.currentTurn = CONFIG.TEAMS.RED;
            scene.uiManager.refreshAfterAction();
            scene.uiManager.updateBottomPanelStatus();
            scene.uiManager.showTurnBanner('ENEMY TURN');
            g.selectedUnit = null;
```

The `refreshAfterAction()` call already triggers `UnitMessageLog.refresh()` (from Step 14a), and since `g.selectedUnit` gets set to null after that call, we need to ensure the icon/panel hide. Move the null assignment **before** the refresh, or add an explicit hide. The simplest approach:

**Replace with:**
```js
            g.currentTurn = CONFIG.TEAMS.RED;
            g.selectedUnit = null;
            g.selectedShip = null;
            scene.uiManager.refreshAfterAction();
            scene.uiManager.updateBottomPanelStatus();
            scene.uiManager.showTurnBanner('ENEMY TURN');
```

Wait — we also need to keep the original `g.selectedShip = null` line. Let me look at the original more carefully.

**Actually, the original code is:**
```js
            g.currentTurn = CONFIG.TEAMS.RED;
            scene.uiManager.refreshAfterAction();
            scene.uiManager.updateBottomPanelStatus();
            scene.uiManager.showTurnBanner('ENEMY TURN');
            g.selectedUnit = null;
            g.selectedShip = null;
            scene.uiManager.updateShipPanelButtons();
            g.activeMode = null;
            scene.uiManager.updateUnitSelector();
            scene.uiManager.refreshAfterAction();
```

Note there are TWO `refreshAfterAction()` calls — the second one at the bottom will run `UnitMessageLog.refresh(scene)` AFTER `g.selectedUnit` is set to null. So the icon/panel will already be hidden automatically. **No change needed here.**

Verify: the flow is:
1. `g.selectedUnit = null;` ← unit deselected
2. `scene.uiManager.refreshAfterAction();` ← triggers `UnitMessageLog.refresh(scene)` which calls `hideIcon(scene)` because `g.selectedUnit` is null.

**This step is already handled. No code change needed.**

---

## Step 18: Handle CameraManager Scroll Blocking

**File:** `managers/CameraManager.js`  

The message panel uses `setScrollFactor(0)` and is not a modal panel, so it doesn't appear in `MODAL_PANEL_KEYS`. We need the wheel handler on the message panel to consume the event before the camera zoom applies. 

The message panel's own `wheel` handler (in `UnitMessageLog.showPanel()`) already captures wheel events when the pointer is over the panel area. However, the CameraManager could still process the zoom. To prevent this, add a check:

**Find (around line 27–48, the wheel handler):**
```js
        scene.input.on('wheel', (pointer, gameObjects, deltaX, deltaY, deltaZ) => {
            // Get pointer position relative to UI camera (Screen Space)
            const uiPoint = pointer.positionToCamera(scene.uiCam);

            // Handle Panel Scrolling (Unit Selection, Locations, Production, etc.)
            const panels = CONFIG.MODAL_PANEL_KEYS.map(key => scene[key]).filter(Boolean);
```

**Replace with:**
```js
        scene.input.on('wheel', (pointer, gameObjects, deltaX, deltaY, deltaZ) => {
            // Get pointer position relative to UI camera (Screen Space)
            const uiPoint = pointer.positionToCamera(scene.uiCam);

            // Block zoom when scrolling over the unit message log panel
            if (scene._msgLogPanel) {
                const px = PANEL_X ?? (CONFIG.UI.SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16);
                const py = 60;
                if (uiPoint.x >= px && uiPoint.x <= px + 340 && uiPoint.y >= py && uiPoint.y <= py + 520) {
                    return; // Consume — UnitMessageLog's own handler will scroll content
                }
            }

            // Handle Panel Scrolling (Unit Selection, Locations, Production, etc.)
            const panels = CONFIG.MODAL_PANEL_KEYS.map(key => scene[key]).filter(Boolean);
```

> Note: Using hardcoded panel bounds here for simplicity. Alternatively, export the constants from `UnitMessageLog.js` and import them.

**Actually, a cleaner approach** — use `getBounds()` like the existing panels do:

**Replace with:**
```js
        scene.input.on('wheel', (pointer, gameObjects, deltaX, deltaY, deltaZ) => {
            // Get pointer position relative to UI camera (Screen Space)
            const uiPoint = pointer.positionToCamera(scene.uiCam);

            // Block zoom when scrolling over the unit message log panel
            if (scene._msgLogPanel && scene._msgLogPanel.visible) {
                const mlX = CONFIG.UI.SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16;
                const mlY = 60;
                if (uiPoint.x >= mlX && uiPoint.x <= mlX + 340 && uiPoint.y >= mlY && uiPoint.y <= mlY + 520) {
                    return;
                }
            }

            // Handle Panel Scrolling (Unit Selection, Locations, Production, etc.)
            const panels = CONFIG.MODAL_PANEL_KEYS.map(key => scene[key]).filter(Boolean);
```

---

## Summary of All File Changes

| # | File | Change Type | Description |
|---|------|-------------|-------------|
| 1 | `managers/ui/UnitMessageLog.js` | **NEW FILE** | Message log helper, icon, panel, scroll, toggle, refresh |
| 2 | `entities/Unit.js` | Constructor | Add `this.messageLog = []` |
| 3 | `entities/Unit.js` | Import | Add `import { addUnitMessage }` |
| 4 | `entities/Unit.js` | `takeDamage()` | Log damage message |
| 5 | `entities/Unit.js` | `heal()` | Log heal message |
| 6 | `entities/Unit.js` | `gainExp()` | Log EXP gain |
| 7 | `entities/Unit.js` | `levelUp()` | Log level-up with stat growth |
| 8 | `entities/Unit.js` | `resetTurn()` | (Optional) Log fort heal |
| 9 | `entities/Hero.js` | Import | Add `import { addUnitMessage }` |
| 10 | `entities/Hero.js` | `levelUp()` | Log hero level-up |
| 11 | `managers/CombatManager.js` | Import | Add `import { addUnitMessage }` |
| 12 | `managers/CombatManager.js` | `finishAction()` | Log attack dealt, defeat, bloodrage, canto, counter, ship damage |
| 13 | `managers/HeroManager.js` | Import | Add `import { addUnitMessage }` |
| 14 | `managers/HeroManager.js` | `_showAbilityText()` | Log ability activation |
| 15 | `managers/HeroManager.js` | Ability cases | Log buff/debuff on target units |
| 16 | `managers/TurnManager.js` | Import | Add `import { addUnitMessage }` |
| 17 | `managers/TurnManager.js` | `showBlockedIndicator()` | Log blocked message |
| 18 | `managers/UIManager.js` | Import | Add `import * as UnitMessageLog` |
| 19 | `managers/UIManager.js` | `refreshAfterAction()` | Call `UnitMessageLog.refresh(scene)` |
| 20 | `managers/InputManager.js` | Import | Add `import * as UnitMessageLog` |
| 21 | `managers/InputManager.js` | `handleEscape()` | Close panel on Escape |
| 22 | `managers/InputManager.js` | `handleTileRightClick()` | Close panel on right-click |
| 23 | `managers/CameraManager.js` | Wheel handler | Block zoom over message panel |

**Total: 1 new file, 8 existing files modified, ~23 individual edit operations.**

---

## Testing Checklist

After implementation, verify:

- [ ] Select a unit → message icon appears at top-left (right of side panel)
- [ ] Deselect unit (click empty tile / Escape / Tab to nothing) → icon disappears
- [ ] Click icon → panel opens with animation, shows "No events recorded." for fresh units
- [ ] Click icon again → panel closes with animation
- [ ] Press Escape → panel closes
- [ ] Right-click anywhere → panel closes
- [ ] Attack an enemy → attacker log shows "Dealt X damage to Y", defender log shows "Took X damage"
- [ ] Counter-attack occurs → both units get counter messages
- [ ] Unit levels up → log shows "Leveled up to Lv N!" with stat growth
- [ ] EXP gained → log shows "+N EXP"
- [ ] Heal unit → log shows "Healed N HP"
- [ ] Hero ability (e.g. Testudo) → hero log shows "TESTUDO activated!", buffed units show "Testudo: +10 DEF..."
- [ ] Bloodrage kill → attacker log shows "Bloodrage activated!"
- [ ] Canto triggers → attacker log shows "Canto: can move after attacking"
- [ ] Blocked unit → log shows "Blocked by adjacent enemy"
- [ ] Fort heal at turn start → log shows "Fort healed N HP"
- [ ] Many messages → scroll works with mouse wheel, content doesn't overflow panel
- [ ] Scroll indicator (▼) appears when content overflows
- [ ] Panel is fixed to screen (doesn't move when map scrolls/zooms)
- [ ] Map zoom doesn't trigger when mouse wheel is over the panel
- [ ] Select different unit while panel is open → panel closes, icon stays for new unit
- [ ] Enemy turn starts → icon and panel disappear
- [ ] Panel styling matches HeroCardPopup / StatBreakdownPanel (dark bg, gold borders)
- [ ] No console errors or visual glitches

---

## Step 19: Log Healer Heal Actions on the Healer

When a healer uses Heal Unit or Heal Area, the **target** already receives a `"Healed X HP"` message via `target.heal()`. However, the **healer** (attacker) gets no log entry about performing the heal. Add messages to the healer so their log shows what they did.

### 19a: Heal Unit — log on healer

**File:** `managers/CombatManager.js`  
**In `finishAction()`**, the `heal_unit` branch:

**Find this code:**
```js
        if (actionType === 'heal_unit' || actionType === 'heal') {
            const finalHeal = Math.floor(target.maxHp * CONFIG.COMBAT.HEAL_UNIT_PERCENT);
            target.heal(finalHeal, '#00ff00');
            scene.uiText.setText(`HEAL UNIT!`);
```

**Replace with:**
```js
        if (actionType === 'heal_unit' || actionType === 'heal') {
            const finalHeal = Math.floor(target.maxHp * CONFIG.COMBAT.HEAL_UNIT_PERCENT);
            target.heal(finalHeal, '#00ff00');
            addUnitMessage(attacker, 'heal', `Healed ${target.classData?.name || 'ally'} for ${finalHeal} HP`, '#00ff00');
            scene.uiText.setText(`HEAL UNIT!`);
```

### 19b: Heal Area — log on healer

**File:** `managers/CombatManager.js`  
**In `executeAreaHeal()`**:

**Find this code:**
```js
            targets.forEach((target) => {
                const healAmount = Math.floor(target.maxHp * areaHealPercent);
                target.heal(healAmount, '#66ff99');
            });

            scene.uiText.setText(`HEAL AREA! (${targets.length} units)`);
```

**Replace with:**
```js
            let totalHealed = 0;
            targets.forEach((target) => {
                const healAmount = Math.floor(target.maxHp * areaHealPercent);
                target.heal(healAmount, '#66ff99');
                totalHealed += healAmount;
            });

            addUnitMessage(healer, 'heal', `Area healed ${targets.length} allies for ${totalHealed} total HP`, '#66ff99');
            scene.uiText.setText(`HEAL AREA! (${targets.length} units)`);
```

---

## Step 20: Log Fort/Location Tile Damage on the Attacker

When a unit damages a fort/port/camp tile (either directly or as collateral when attacking a unit on a fort), log the tile damage on the attacker's message log.

### 20a: Direct attack on empty fort/port/camp tile

**File:** `managers/CombatManager.js`  
**In `finishAction()`**, the `target.isLocationTarget` branch:

**Find this code (after `loc.tileHp[key]` is reduced):**
```js
                loc.tileHp[key] = Math.max(0, (loc.tileHp[key] || 0) - fortDamage);

                const worldX = (target.gridX + 0.5) * CONFIG.TILE_SIZE;
```

**Replace with:**
```js
                loc.tileHp[key] = Math.max(0, (loc.tileHp[key] || 0) - fortDamage);
                addUnitMessage(attacker, 'damage', `Dealt ${fortDamage} damage to ${tile.type} tile`, '#ff3333');

                const worldX = (target.gridX + 0.5) * CONFIG.TILE_SIZE;
```

### 20b: Collateral fort tile damage when attacking unit on fort

**File:** `managers/CombatManager.js`  
**In `finishAction()`**, the collateral section:

After `loc.tileHp[key]` is reduced, add:
```js
addUnitMessage(attacker, 'damage', `Dealt ${tileDamage} collateral damage to ${targetTile.type} tile`, '#ff6633');
```

### 20c: Counter-attack collateral fort tile damage

**File:** `managers/CombatManager.js`  
**In `finishAction()`**, the counter section:

After counter collateral `loc.tileHp[key]` is reduced, add:
```js
addUnitMessage(target, 'damage', `Counter-attack dealt ${counterEffectiveDamage} collateral damage to ${attackerTile.type} tile`, '#ff6633');
```

---

## Step 21: Add `messageLog` to Ships

Ships are not `Unit` instances — they are plain objects built by `ShipPlacement.buildShipData()`. To let ships maintain a message log, add `messageLog: []` to the ship data object. The `addUnitMessage()` helper already guards with `if (!unit || !unit.messageLog) return;`, so it will work on any object with a `messageLog` array.

### 21a: Add `messageLog` to ship data

**File:** `managers/naval/ShipPlacement.js`  
**In `buildShipData()`**:

**Find this code:**
```js
            ballistaShotsUsed: 0,
            firedBallistaTileIndicesThisTurn: []
        };
```

**Replace with:**
```js
            ballistaShotsUsed: 0,
            firedBallistaTileIndicesThisTurn: [],
            messageLog: []
        };
```

### 21b: Import and log in ShipCombat.js

**File:** `managers/naval/ShipCombat.js`  
Add `import { addUnitMessage } from '../ui/UnitMessageLog.js';` and log on:

- `executeBombard()`: both attacker and target ship
- `executeBombardUnit()`: on the attacker ship
- `executeBombardLocation()`: on the attacker ship

### 21c: Import and log in ShipState.js

**File:** `managers/naval/ShipState.js`  
Add `import { addUnitMessage } from '../ui/UnitMessageLog.js';` and log on:

- `startSinking()`: ship sinking message
- `repairDockedShipsForTeam()`: jetty repair message
- `claimDerelictWithUnit()`: derelict claim message

### 21d: Log splash damage to ship in CombatManager

**File:** `managers/CombatManager.js`  
When attacking a unit on deck and 50% splash goes to the ship, log on both the ship and the attacker.

---
