## Updated Testing Checklist

After implementation, verify all original items plus:

- [ ] Healer uses Heal Unit → healer's log shows "Healed [unit name] for X HP"
- [ ] Healer uses Heal Area → healer's log shows "Area healed N allies for X total HP"
- [ ] Attack empty fort tile → attacker's log shows "Dealt X damage to FORT tile"
- [ ] Attack unit on fort → attacker's log shows collateral: "Dealt X collateral damage to FORT tile"
- [ ] Counter-attack on fort → counter-attacker's log shows collateral tile damage
- [ ] Ship has messageLog array (check in console: `ship.messageLog`)
- [ ] Ship bombards enemy ship → both ships' logs show bombard damage
- [ ] Ship bombards unit → ship's log shows "Bombard dealt X damage to [unit]"
- [ ] Ship bombards fort tile → ship's log shows "Bombard dealt X damage to FORT tile"
- [ ] Ship starts sinking → ship's log shows "Ship is sinking!"
- [ ] Ship repaired at jetty → ship's log shows "Jetty repaired X hull HP"
- [ ] Derelict ship claimed → ship's log shows "Ship claimed by [unit]"
- [ ] Attack unit on deck → ship's log shows splash damage, attacker's log shows splash dealt
- [ ] No console errors or visual glitches

---

## Execution Notes for Implementing Models

1. **Execute steps in order** — imports must be added before the code that uses them.
2. **Use exact string matching** when finding code to replace. Include 3–5 lines of context around each edit target.
3. **Line numbers are approximate** — always match by code content, not line number, as other edits may shift lines.
4. **The new file (Step 1) should be created first** since all other files import from it.
5. **Test incrementally** — after Steps 1–7 (data layer + Unit instrumentation), you can verify that `unit.messageLog` accumulates entries by logging in the console. After Steps 14–16 (UI wiring), the panel should be fully functional.
6. **If a find/replace fails**, read the target file to get the current exact code — another step may have shifted surrounding lines.


## Documentation: unit-message-mgmt.md
# Unit Message Log — Design & Implementation Plan

## Overview

Add a per-unit **Message Log** that captures every floating text, combat result, level-up, buff, ability activation, and other transient event that affects a unit during a game. The log is accessed through a small **message-box icon** that appears when a unit is selected, positioned in the upper-left area of the map viewport (right of the side panel). Clicking the icon opens a scrollable, styled panel showing the full history for that unit. The panel closes on a second click of the icon, pressing **Escape**, or **right-clicking** anywhere.

---

## Goals

| # | Goal |
|---|------|
| 1 | Store every transient message that relates to a unit (damage dealt/taken, heals, EXP, level-ups, ability activations, counter-attacks, hero buffs, gold, blocked indicators, status effects). |
| 2 | Let the player review the full history for a selected unit at any time. |
| 3 | Keep the UI unobtrusive — a small icon that only appears when a unit is selected. |
| 4 | Panel appears in the **upper-left corner** of the screen, fixed to the camera (no scroll/zoom). |
| 5 | Match the existing medieval panel styling (brown/gold/dark-inner-border pattern). |
| 6 | Messages are color-coded by category for readability. |

---

## Data Model

### `Unit.messageLog` Array

Each `Unit` instance gets a new property initialized in the constructor:

```js
this.messageLog = [];   // Array<MessageEntry>
```

#### `MessageEntry` Shape

```js
{
    turn:      Number,      // game turn when the message occurred (g.turnCount)
    category:  String,      // 'damage' | 'heal' | 'exp' | 'levelup' | 'ability' | 'counter' |
                            // 'buff' | 'status' | 'gold' | 'blocked' | 'canto' | 'misc'
    text:      String,      // human-readable message, e.g. "Took 14 damage from Orc Spear"
    color:     String,      // hex color string for display, e.g. '#ff0000'
}
```

### Category Color Map

Re-uses existing floating-text colors where possible:

| Category   | Color       | Matches                     |
|------------|-------------|-----------------------------|
| `damage`   | `#ff0000`   | Damage text (red)           |
| `heal`     | `#00ff00`   | Heal text (green)           |
| `exp`      | `#88ccff`   | EXP gain text (light blue)  |
| `levelup`  | `#ffd700`   | Level-up text (gold)        |
| `ability`  | `#ffd700`   | Hero ability text (gold)    |
| `counter`  | `#ff8844`   | Counter-attack text (orange)|
| `buff`     | `#88ddff`   | Aura/magic color            |
| `status`   | `#ff8844`   | Status effect color         |
| `gold`     | `#ffd700`   | Gold gain text              |
| `blocked`  | `#ffcc00`   | Blocked indicator (yellow)  |
| `canto`    | `#ffff00`   | Canto text (yellow)         |
| `misc`     | `#cccccc`   | Fallback (light gray)       |

---

## Message Collection — Instrumentation Points

Every place that currently creates floating text should **also** push to the acting unit's `messageLog`. A helper function `addUnitMessage(unit, category, text, color)` keeps instrumentation one-liners.

### Helper (new file: `managers/ui/UnitMessageLog.js`)

```js
/**
 * Push a message entry onto a unit's message log.
 * @param {Unit} unit
 * @param {String} category
 * @param {String} text
 * @param {String} color   – hex color for display
 */
export function addUnitMessage(unit, category, text, color) {
    if (!unit || !unit.messageLog) return;
    const g = unit.scene?.gameState ?? unit.scene?.game?.gameState;
    unit.messageLog.push({
        turn: g?.turnCount ?? 0,
        category,
        text,
        color: color || '#cccccc',
    });
}
```

### Instrumentation Sites

| File | Method / Line Area | Category | Example Message |
|------|--------------------|----------|-----------------|
| `entities/Unit.js` | `takeDamage()` — after `finalAmount` computed | `damage` | `"Took 14 damage"` or `"Took 14 damage (CRIT!)"` |
| `entities/Unit.js` | `heal()` — after amount clamped | `heal` | `"Healed 8 HP"` |
| `entities/Unit.js` | `gainExp()` | `exp` | `"+25 EXP"` |
| `entities/Unit.js` | `levelUp()` | `levelup` | `"Leveled up to Lv 3!"` |
| `entities/Hero.js` | `levelUp()` | `levelup` | `"Hero leveled up to Lv 4!"` |
| `managers/CombatManager.js` | `finishAction()` — counter section | `counter` | `"Counter-attacked for 6 damage"` |
| `managers/CombatManager.js` | `finishAction()` — CANTO section | `canto` | `"Canto: can move after attacking"` |
| `managers/CombatManager.js` | `finishAction()` — BLOODRAGE section | `ability` | `"Bloodrage activated!"` |
| `managers/CombatManager.js` | ship damage | `damage` | `"Ship took 12 damage"` |
| `managers/HeroManager.js` | `_showAbilityText()` | `ability` | `"Testudo activated!"` / `"Blessing!"` |
| `managers/HeroManager.js` | aura/buff application | `buff` | `"Rally aura: +2 ATK"` |
| `managers/TurnManager.js` | `showBlockedIndicator()` | `blocked` | `"Blocked by adjacent enemy"` |
| `managers/UIManager.js` | `showFloatingGoldText()` | `gold` | `"+50 Gold"` |
| `managers/CombatManager.js` | attacker damage dealt | `damage` | `"Dealt 14 damage to Orc Spear"` |
| `managers/CombatManager.js` | defender killed | `misc` | `"Defeated Orc Spear"` |

> **Principle**: Always add the message to the **affected** unit. For combat, the attacker gets a "Dealt X damage to Y" message AND the defender gets a "Took X damage from Z" message.

---

## UI Components

### 1. Message-Box Icon (Toggle Button)

- **Appearance**: Small `36×36` icon positioned at **(SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16, 16)** — just right of the side panel, upper-left corner. Fixed to camera (`setScrollFactor(0)`).
- **Visual**: A rounded rectangle with the panel background color (`PANEL_BG`) and gold trim (`PANEL_TRIM`), containing a small "scroll" or "envelope" glyph drawn with Phaser Graphics (3 horizontal lines icon representing a message list).
- **Depth**: `3150` — above the map but below modal panels and the side panel's popout cards.
- **Visibility**: Hidden by default. Shown when `g.selectedUnit` is set (called from `UIManager.refreshAfterAction()` or unit selection). Hidden when selection is cleared.
- **Interaction**: On `pointerdown`, toggle the message log panel open/closed.
- **Hover**: Slight brightness increase + cursor change, matching the existing button hover pattern.

#### Icon Design (Phaser Graphics)

```
┌──────────┐
│  ═══════ │   ← 3 horizontal gold lines
│  ═══════ │     inside a small brown rounded rect
│  ═════   │     with gold outer border
└──────────┘
```

```js
// Draw icon background
const icon = scene.add.container(SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16, 16)
    .setScrollFactor(0).setDepth(3150);

const bg = scene.add.graphics();
bg.fillStyle(PANEL_BG, 1);
bg.fillRoundedRect(0, 0, 36, 36, 8);
bg.lineStyle(2, PANEL_TRIM, 1);
bg.strokeRoundedRect(0, 0, 36, 36, 8);

// Three horizontal lines (message icon)
bg.lineStyle(2, 0xffd700, 0.9);
bg.beginPath();
bg.moveTo(8, 10);  bg.lineTo(28, 10);
bg.moveTo(8, 18);  bg.lineTo(28, 18);
bg.moveTo(8, 26);  bg.lineTo(22, 26);  // shorter third line
bg.strokePath();

icon.add(bg);

// Hit area
const hitZone = scene.add.zone(18, 18, 36, 36).setInteractive({ useHandCursor: true });
icon.add(hitZone);
```

### 2. Message Log Panel

- **Position**: Fixed to camera. Upper-left corner, anchored at **(SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16, 56)** — directly below the icon.
- **Size**: **340 × 520** pixels (roughly). Width fits cleanly between the side panel and center of the screen. Height gives room for many messages. If there are more messages than fit, the panel supports **mouse-wheel scrolling**.
- **Depth**: `3160` — above the icon, below modal panels.
- **`setScrollFactor(0)`** on all elements + `scene.cameras.main.ignore()` so it never moves with the map.

#### Panel Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│  ╔═══════════════════════════╗  │  ← Gold outer border (6px)
│  ║  ╔═════════════════════╗  ║  │  ← Dark inner border (2px)
│  ║  ║  📜 Unit Name — Log ║  ║  │  ← Title bar (gold text, 24px bold)
│  ║  ║─────────────────────║  ║  │  ← Thin gold separator line
│  ║  ║  Turn 5             ║  ║  │  ← Turn header (gold, 16px bold)
│  ║  ║  Took 14 damage     ║  ║  │  ← Message (category color, 16px)
│  ║  ║  Counter! 6 damage  ║  ║  │
│  ║  ║  +25 EXP            ║  ║  │
│  ║  ║                     ║  ║  │
│  ║  ║  Turn 4             ║  ║  │
│  ║  ║  Healed 8 HP        ║  ║  │
│  ║  ║  Rally aura: +2 ATK ║  ║  │
│  ║  ║  ...                ║  ║  │
│  ║  ╚═════════════════════╝  ║  │
│  ╚═══════════════════════════╝  │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### Panel Styling (matches existing pattern)

```js
const PANEL_W = 340;
const PANEL_H = 520;
const panel = scene.add.container(SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16, 56)
    .setScrollFactor(0).setDepth(3160);
scene.cameras.main.ignore(panel);

const bg = scene.add.graphics();
// Fill
bg.fillStyle(0x0f0b07, 0.95);               // Near-black (matches popout cards)
bg.fillRoundedRect(0, 0, PANEL_W, PANEL_H, 14);
// Gold outer border
bg.lineStyle(4, CONFIG.UI.COLORS.PANEL_TRIM, 1);
bg.strokeRoundedRect(0, 0, PANEL_W, PANEL_H, 14);
// Dark inner border
bg.lineStyle(2, CONFIG.UI.COLORS.PANEL_INNER_STROKE, 0.8);
bg.strokeRoundedRect(8, 8, PANEL_W - 16, PANEL_H - 16, 10);
panel.add(bg);

// Title
const title = scene.add.text(PANEL_W / 2, 22, `${unit.name} — Message Log`, {
    fontSize: '20px', fill: '#ffd700', fontStyle: 'bold',
    stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 3
}).setOrigin(0.5, 0);
panel.add(title);

// Separator line (gold)
const sep = scene.add.graphics();
sep.lineStyle(1, 0xffd700, 0.6);
sep.beginPath();
sep.moveTo(20, 48);
sep.lineTo(PANEL_W - 20, 48);
sep.strokePath();
panel.add(sep);
```

#### Message Rendering

Messages are rendered newest-first (most recent at top) so the player immediately sees what just happened. Messages are grouped by turn with a small **turn header**.

```js
// Pseudo-code for rendering messages
const CONTENT_TOP = 54;
const LINE_HEIGHT = 20;
const MAX_VISIBLE_LINES = Math.floor((PANEL_H - CONTENT_TOP - 16) / LINE_HEIGHT);
let yOffset = CONTENT_TOP;
let lastTurn = -1;

// Reverse iterate (newest first)
const entries = [...unit.messageLog].reverse();
for (const entry of entries) {
    if (entry.turn !== lastTurn) {
        // Turn header
        const turnHeader = scene.add.text(20, yOffset, `— Turn ${entry.turn} —`, {
            fontSize: '14px', fill: '#ffd700', fontStyle: 'bold',
            stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 2
        });
        panel.add(turnHeader);
        yOffset += LINE_HEIGHT;
        lastTurn = entry.turn;
    }

    const msg = scene.add.text(24, yOffset, entry.text, {
        fontSize: '15px', fill: entry.color,
        stroke: '#000000', strokeThickness: 1,
        wordWrap: { width: PANEL_W - 56 }
    });
    panel.add(msg);
    yOffset += msg.height + 4;
}
```

#### Scrolling (Mouse Wheel)

If the total message content exceeds the panel height, support scrolling:

- Mask the content area using a Phaser `Graphics` geometry mask so text doesn't overflow the panel bounds.
- Track a `scrollOffset` variable.
- On `wheel` event over the panel, adjust `scrollOffset` and reposition the content container.
- Clamp so the player can't scroll past the top or bottom.

```js
// Create a mask for the content area
const maskShape = scene.make.graphics();
maskShape.fillRect(panelX + 16, panelY + CONTENT_TOP, PANEL_W - 32, PANEL_H - CONTENT_TOP - 16);
const mask = maskShape.createGeometryMask();
contentContainer.setMask(mask);

// Scroll handler
scene.input.on('wheel', (pointer, gameObjects, deltaX, deltaY) => {
    if (!panelVisible) return;
    // Check if pointer is over the panel
    const localX = pointer.x - panelX;
    const localY = pointer.y - panelY;
    if (localX >= 0 && localX <= PANEL_W && localY >= 0 && localY <= PANEL_H) {
        scrollOffset = Phaser.Math.Clamp(
            scrollOffset - deltaY * 0.5,
            -(totalContentHeight - visibleHeight),
            0
        );
        contentContainer.y = CONTENT_TOP + scrollOffset;
    }
});
```

#### Scroll Indicator

When content overflows, show a subtle gold scroll indicator (small triangle or "▼ more" text) at the bottom of the panel to hint that the player can scroll down.

---

## Interaction & Lifecycle

### Opening

1. Player **selects a unit** (click on map, Tab cycle, etc.)
2. `UIManager.refreshAfterAction()` calls `UnitMessageLog.showIcon(scene, unit)`.
3. The 36×36 icon appears at `(SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16, 16)`.

### Toggling the Panel

1. Player **clicks the icon**.
2. If panel is closed → call `UnitMessageLog.showPanel(scene, unit)`:
   - Build the panel container with all messages.
   - Animate in with a quick tween (alpha 0→1, y offset -10→0, duration 150ms, ease `Sine.easeOut`).
3. If panel is open → call `UnitMessageLog.hidePanel(scene)`:
   - Animate out (alpha 1→0, duration 100ms), then destroy.

### Closing

The panel closes (calls `hidePanel`) when **any** of these occur:

| Trigger | Where to Hook |
|---------|---------------|
| Click the icon again | Icon `pointerdown` handler |
| Press **Escape** | `InputManager.handleEscape()` — check before modal panels |
| **Right-click** anywhere | `InputManager` pointer handler or scene `pointerdown` with `event.button === 2` |
| Select a **different unit** | `UIManager.refreshAfterAction()` → hide old panel, show icon for new unit |
| **Deselect** unit (click empty tile) | `InputManager.clearSelection()` → hide icon + panel |
| Start of **enemy turn** | `TurnManager.endTurn()` → hide icon + panel |

### Scene Key for Escape

Store the panel on the scene as `scene._unitMessagePanel` and the icon as `scene._unitMessageIcon`. Add a check in `InputManager.handleEscape()` **before** the `MODAL_PANEL_KEYS` loop:

```js
if (scene._unitMessagePanel) {
    UnitMessageLog.hidePanel(scene);
    return;
}
```

This avoids needing to add it to `MODAL_PANEL_KEYS` since it's not a full modal — it doesn't block map interaction.

---

## File Structure

### New File: `managers/ui/UnitMessageLog.js`

Single module containing:

| Export | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `addUnitMessage(unit, category, text, color)` | Push entry to `unit.messageLog` |
| `showIcon(scene, unit)` | Create/show the toggle icon |
| `hideIcon(scene)` | Hide and destroy the icon |
| `showPanel(scene, unit)` | Build and display the message panel |
| `hidePanel(scene)` | Animate out and destroy the panel |
| `togglePanel(scene, unit)` | Toggle between show/hide |
| `clearLog(unit)` | Empty a unit's log (for new game / reset) |

---

## Message Log Persistence

- The message log lives on the `Unit` instance (`unit.messageLog`).
- It is **not saved** to disk — it resets each game session. This keeps save files small and avoids stale data. (Can be revisited later if save/load of logs is desired.)
- On unit death, the log is lost. This is acceptable — dead units can't be selected.

---

## Depth Map (Context)

| Layer | Depth | Element |
|-------|-------|---------|
| Map tiles / units | 0–1000 | – |
| Floating text | 2000–2005 | Damage, heal, EXP, level-up |
| Ability text | 3000 | Counter, Canto, Bloodrage |
| Side panel | 3100 | `scene.sidePanel` |
| **Message icon** | **3150** | New |
| **Message panel** | **3160** | New |
| Stat breakdown / Hero card popout | 3199–3200 | Existing |
| Medieval toast | 4000 | Existing |
| Modal blocker | 6998 | Existing |
| Modal panels | 6999 | Existing |

The message panel sits between the side panel and the stat popouts, which is correct since it should be below any modals or detailed popout cards.

---

## Visual Design Specifications

### Icon

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Size | 36 × 36 px |
| Position | `(SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16, 16)` = `(400, 16)` |
| Background | `PANEL_BG` (`0x5c4033`) fill, `PANEL_TRIM` (`0xc5a059`) 2px stroke, rounded 8px |
| Glyph | 3 horizontal gold lines (message/list icon), stroked at 2px, color `0xffd700` |
| Hover | Background lightens to `0x7a5a43`, gold lines brighten to `0xffe066` |
| ScrollFactor | 0 (fixed to camera) |
| Depth | 3150 |

### Panel

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Size | 340 × 520 px |
| Position | `(SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH + 16, 56)` = `(400, 56)` |
| Background | `0x0f0b07` at 95% opacity (near-black, matches HeroCardPopup / StatBreakdownPanel) |
| Outer border | 4px `PANEL_TRIM` (`0xc5a059`) gold |
| Inner border | 2px `PANEL_INNER_STROKE` (`0x2a1a12`) at 80% alpha |
| Corner radius | 14px outer, 10px inner |
| Title | `"UnitName — Log"`, 20px, `#ffd700`, bold, stroke `#000` 3px, centered |
| Separator | 1px gold line at y=48, 60% alpha |
| Turn headers | `"— Turn N —"`, 14px, `#ffd700`, bold, stroke `#000` 2px |
| Messages | 15px, category color, stroke `#000` 1px, word-wrap at 284px |
| Line spacing | 4px between messages, 8px before turn headers |
| Scroll | Mouse wheel, clamped, geometry mask on content area |
| Scroll indicator | Small `"▼"` gold text at bottom-right when content overflows |
| Animation in | Alpha 0→1, y -10→0, 150ms, `Sine.easeOut` |
| Animation out | Alpha 1→0, 100ms |
| ScrollFactor | 0 (fixed to camera) |
| Depth | 3160 |

### Message Text Examples

| Turn | Message | Color |
|------|---------|-------|
| 5 | `"Dealt 14 damage to Orc Spear"` | `#ff0000` |
| 5 | `"Took 6 counter-attack damage"` | `#ff8844` |
| 5 | `"+25 EXP"` | `#88ccff` |
| 4 | `"Rally aura: +2 ATK"` | `#88ddff` |
| 4 | `"Healed 8 HP"` | `#00ff00` |
| 3 | `"Leveled up to Lv 3!"` | `#ffd700` |
| 3 | `"Testudo activated!"` | `#ffd700` |
| 2 | `"Took 11 damage from Orc Rider"` | `#ff0000` |
| 2 | `"Blocked by adjacent enemy"` | `#ffcc00` |
| 1 | `"+50 Gold"` | `#ffd700` |

---

## Implementation Steps

### Phase 1 — Data Layer

1. Add `this.messageLog = []` to `Unit` constructor.
2. Create `managers/ui/UnitMessageLog.js` with the `addUnitMessage()` helper.
3. Instrument all floating-text creation sites (listed above) with `addUnitMessage()` calls.

### Phase 2 — Icon

4. Add `showIcon()` / `hideIcon()` functions to `UnitMessageLog.js`.
5. Call `showIcon(scene, unit)` from `UIManager.refreshAfterAction()` when a unit is selected.
6. Call `hideIcon(scene)` from selection-clear / turn-change paths.

### Phase 3 — Panel

7. Add `showPanel()` / `hidePanel()` / `togglePanel()` functions.
8. Wire icon click → `togglePanel()`.
9. Implement message rendering with turn grouping, newest-first order, and color-coding.
10. Implement geometry mask + mouse-wheel scrolling.
11. Add scroll indicator.

### Phase 4 — Dismissal

12. Add Escape check in `InputManager.handleEscape()`.
13. Add right-click dismissal in `InputManager`.
14. Ensure panel hides on unit deselection (already handled via `hideIcon` calls).

### Phase 5 — Polish

15. Add open/close tween animations.
16. Add hover effect on icon.
17. Test with units that have many messages (scroll behavior).
18. Test panel position doesn't overlap side panel at various screen sizes.
19. Verify messages appear correctly for all combat scenarios (melee, ranged, siege, naval).
20. Ensure no depth conflicts with existing panels.

---

## Edge Cases

| Case | Handling |
|------|----------|
| Unit has no messages | Show panel with centered italic text: `"No events recorded."` in `#888888` |
| Very long message text | Word-wrap at 284px; height adjusts dynamically per message |
| Hundreds of messages | Scroll support; content is rendered in a container that extends beyond the mask. Consider capping at ~200 entries and dropping oldest if performance is a concern. |
| Unit dies mid-combat | Log still exists on the Unit object but can't be viewed (unit can't be selected). Acceptable. |
| Multiple combats in one turn | All messages share the same turn header — they appear in chronological order within the turn group. |
| Panel open + enemy turn starts | Auto-close panel and hide icon on turn change. |
| Panel open + click different unit | Close panel, show icon for new unit. If player clicks icon again, new unit's log is shown. |

---

## Future Enhancements (Out of Scope for V1)

- **Filter by category**: Tabs or toggle buttons to show only damage, heals, buffs, etc.
- **Export / copy log**: Button to copy the log text to clipboard.
- **Persist in save**: Include `messageLog` in save data for post-session review.
- **Dead unit log**: Allow clicking a grave marker to see the fallen unit's log.
- **Notification badge**: Small red badge on the icon showing the count of unread messages since last opened.
- **AI unit logs**: Allow viewing enemy unit logs after selecting them (read-only intel).


## Documentation: unit-stat-design.md
# Unit Stat Design

This document describes how unit stats (HP, ATK, RNG, DEF, MOV) are modified by heroes, terrain, abilities, and other factors, and proposes ways to surface those modifiers to the player.

---

## 1. How Each Stat Is Modified

### 1.1 Heroes

#### Hero base (bonus stats)
- **Source:** `CONFIG.HEROES[].bonusStats` applied in the `Hero` constructor (`entities/Hero.js`).
- **Stats:** HP, ATK, DEF, MOV (and `rng` in config; range is applied via aura / `getEffectiveRange()`).
- **Effect:** Flat addition to the base class. Each hero has one set (e.g. Marcus: +15 HP, +5 ATK, +3 DEF, +1 MOV).

#### Hero aura (passive, in range)
- **Source:** `HeroManager.getAuraBonus()`; auras do not stack per stat (best single-hero bonus per stat).
- **Formula:** `min(base + floor((level - 1) / 3), cap)` per hero; then take the max across heroes. **RNG:** The effective aura bonus is further capped at **+2** for all heroes (`CONFIG.LEVELING.AURA_RNG_CAP`); applies to ranged units only.
- **Stats:** HP, ATK, DEF, MOV, RNG.
- **Effect:** Only friendly units (and the hero) within `heroData.aura.range` receive the bonus. Used in `Unit.getEffectiveAtk/Def/Mov/MaxHp/Range()`.

#### Hero abilities (status effects / one-shot)
- **TESTUDO (Marcus):** +10 DEF, MOV clamped to 2, no crits (status: `defBonus`, `movOverride`, `noCrit`).
- **BULWARK (Valeria):** +8 DEF (allies) / +12 DEF (self) (status: `defBonus`).
- **LUNGE (Roderic):** Next attack 2× ATK (status: `atkMultiplier`); Canto grants 2 MOV after kill.
- **RUNEBLAST (Alana):** Target −4 ATK for 2 turns (status: `atkReduction`).
- **BLESSING (Liora):** Target +15 DEF (status: `defBonus`).
- **FORTIFY (Gaius):** Units on location +4 ATK, +4 DEF (status: `atkBonus`, `defBonus`).
- **BLOODRAGE (Brennus):** Next attack 2× ATK (status: `atkMultiplier`); no direct stat change.
- **MAELSTROM (Thalassa):** Frozen = MOV 0 for 1 turn (status: `movOverride: 0`).

---

### 1.2 Terrain (tile under the unit)

#### Defense (DEF)
- **Source:** `CONFIG.TERRAIN[].defenseBonus`; applied in combat when resolving damage (`CombatManager`, `ShipCombat`).
- **Tiles:** Fort (+3), Castra (+2), Ship Deck (+0.1).
- **Effect:** Added to the defender’s effective DEF for damage calculation only (not to the unit’s printed DEF).

#### Damage taken (effective toughness)
- **Damage reduction:** `CONFIG.TERRAIN[].damageReduction` in `Unit.takeDamage()` and combat.
  - Forest: 25%; Fort: 50% (only while tile has HP); Castra: 25%.
- **Damage bonus (increased damage taken):** `CONFIG.TERRAIN[].damageBonus`.
  - Bridge: +25% damage taken.
- **Effect:** Multipliers on incoming damage; they do not change the unit’s ATK/DEF stats, only effective toughness.

#### HP recovery (not a stat modifier)
- **Source:** `CONFIG.TERRAIN[].startTurnHeal` applied at turn start in `Unit`.
- **Tiles:** Fort 20%, Port 10%, Castra 10%.
- **Effect:** Heals a % of max HP; does not change max HP or other stats.

---

### 1.3 Unit class / unit-type abilities

#### Class damage reduction and bonus (by attack type)
- **Source:** `CONFIG.UNIT_CLASSES[].damageReduction` and `damageBonus` (e.g. `damageReduction: { magic: 0.5 }`).
- **Used in:** `CombatManager` when applying damage (e.g. magic damage reduced 50% for Healer/Mage).
- **Effect:** Acts like extra “DEF” vs specific damage types (physical/magic/siege); does not change printed DEF.

#### Class-based incoming damage factor (`receiveDamageFactorFrom`)
- **Source:** `CONFIG.UNIT_CLASSES[].receiveDamageFactorFrom` (e.g. Spearman: `[{ attackerKey: 'unit-knight', factor: 0.5 }]`).
- **Used in:** `CombatManager` after computing final damage — multiplies total incoming damage by `factor`.
- **Units:** Spearman takes 50% damage **from** Knight/OrcRider. This is a defender-side reduction keyed to the attacker's class.
- **Effect:** Flat multiplier on damage received; does not change any printed stat.

#### Class vs unit (bonus damage, not ATK)
- **Source:** `CONFIG.UNIT_CLASSES[].bonusAgainst` (e.g. Knight: +5 vs Archer/Healer/Mage).
- **Used in:** `CombatManager` — added to `atkPower` before damage calculation.
- **Effect:** Flat damage addition in combat; does not change the unit's printed ATK stat.

#### Class vs location (bonus damage %)
- **Source:** `CONFIG.UNIT_CLASSES[].bonusAgainstLocation` (e.g. Catapult: `[{ type: 'FORT', bonusPercent: 100 }, { type: 'PORT', bonusPercent: 100 }]`).
- **Used in:** `CombatManager` — multiplies `atkPower` by `(1 + bonusPercent/100)` when attacking a location tile.
- **Effect:** Percentage damage bonus against fort/port tiles; does not change printed ATK.

---

### 1.4 Status effects (temporary)

- **Source:** `Unit.statusEffects`; applied in `Unit.getEffectiveAtk/Def/Mov()` and `getAtkMultiplier()` / no-crit checks.
- **ATK:** `atkBonus`, `atkReduction`, `atkMultiplier` (e.g. BLOODRAGE 2×, RUNEBLAST −4 ATK).
- **DEF:** `defBonus` (TESTUDO, BULWARK, BLESSING, FORTIFY).
- **MOV:** `movOverride` (TESTUDO → clamp to 2; MAELSTROM freeze → 0).
- **Other:** `noCrit` (TESTUDO), `damageOnMove` (VOLLEY bleed; HP loss, not stat).

---

### 1.5 Combat action (Defend)

- **Source:** `CONFIG.COMBAT.DEFEND_BONUS` (2); applied when `target.isDefending` or `attacker.isDefending` in `CombatManager`.
- **Effect:** +2 DEF for that combat instance only (incoming and counter-attack); not a permanent stat change.

---

### 1.6 Level-up (growth)

- **Source:** `Unit.levelUp()` / `Hero.levelUp()` using `classData.growth` or `heroData.growth`.
- **Stats:** HP (maxHp), ATK, DEF only (no MOV/RNG from growth).
- **Effect:** Permanent increase when gaining a level (EXP threshold).

---

### 1.7 Orc Frenzy

- **Source:** `CombatManager` — orc-team units only.
- **Formula:** `+floor(missingHp / 5)` added to `atkPower` in combat (both attack and counter-attack).
- **Effect:** Flat ATK bonus in combat that scales with missing HP. Not reflected in `getEffectiveAtk()` — it is a combat-only modifier.

---

### 1.8 Crit / variance system

- **Source:** Random roll in `CombatManager` before damage is applied.
- **Distribution:** 1.0× (60%), 1.10× (15%), 1.25× (5%), 0.75× (5%), 0.90× (15%).
- **Suppression:** `noCrit` status effect (e.g. TESTUDO) clamps the multiplier to ≤ 1.0, preventing any positive variance.
- **Effect:** Multiplier on final damage; not a stat modifier.

---

### 1.9 Counter-attack damage factor

- **Source:** `CONFIG.COMBAT.COUNTER_ATTACK_DAMAGE_FACTOR` (default 0.5).
- **Used in:** `CombatManager` — counter-attack final damage is multiplied by this factor.
- **Effect:** Counter-attacks deal 50% of normal damage. Stacks with all other modifiers (class reduction, flanking, etc.).

#### Flanked counter-attack penalty
- **Source:** `CombatManager` — computed from the **defender's** (counter-attacker's) flank count.
- **Formula:** Counter-attack damage is further reduced by the defender's flank count:
  - 1 adjacent enemy: counter-attack damage × 0.75 (−25%)
  - 2 adjacent enemies: counter-attack damage × 0.50 (−50%)
  - 3 adjacent enemies: counter-attack damage × 0.25 (−75%)
  - 4+ adjacent enemies: counter-attack **suppressed** (0 damage, no counter occurs)
- **Effect:** Flanked units are weaker at counter-attacking. At 4+ flanks, counter-attacks are completely suppressed. This stacks multiplicatively with the base 0.5× counter-attack factor.

---

### 1.10 Engineer & Catapult special rules

#### Engineer 3× vs Fort tiles
- **Source:** `CombatManager` — when an engineer attacks an unoccupied fort tile, `atkPower *= 3`.
- **Effect:** Engineers are the primary fort-breaker for unoccupied tiles.

#### Catapult 2× tile HP damage
- **Source:** `CombatManager` — `damageToFortTile = finalDamage * 2` for catapult.
- **Effect:** Catapults deal double damage to the fort tile's HP (separate from damage to the unit on the tile).

#### Catapult skips Fort terrain reduction
- **Source:** `CombatManager` sets `skipTerrainReduction = true` for catapult vs fort tile.
- **Effect:** The fort's 50% terrain damage reduction does not apply to catapult attacks on the tile itself.

---

### 1.11 Fort tile HP gate on damage reduction

- **Source:** `Unit.takeDamage()` — checks `loc.tileHp[key]` before applying `CONFIG.TERRAIN[].damageReduction`.
- **Effect:** Fort's 50% damage reduction **only applies while the tile has HP > 0**. Once the tile is destroyed, units standing on it lose the terrain protection.

---

### 1.12 Deck unit → ship splash damage

- **Source:** `CombatManager` — when a unit on a ship deck takes damage, 50% of that damage is also applied to the ship's hull HP.
- **Effect:** Attacking deck units also damages the ship. This is one-directional (ship damage does not splash back to the unit).

---

### 1.13 BLOODRAGE post-combat effects

- **Source:** `CombatManager` `doEndOfCombat` block; consumed after the attack resolves.
- **On kill:** Heal `killHealPercent` of max HP; if `bonusAttack` is true, gain 2 bonus moves (Canto-style) with actions restricted to movement + attack.
- **On miss (target survives):** Self-damage equal to `failSelfDamagePercent` of max HP.
- **Effect:** These are post-combat consequences, not stat modifiers. The `atkMultiplier: 2` is the stat-visible part (§1.4).

---

### 1.14 Combat-only summary (damage, not stats)

- **Flanking:** Extra damage % based on flank count: 2 sides (+25%), 3 sides (+50%), 4+ sides (+100%) in `CombatManager`; does not change ATK/DEF.
- **Terrain `defenseBonus`:** Added to defender's DEF in combat only (Fort +3, Castra +2, Ship Deck +0.1).
- **Terrain `damageReduction`:** Multiplier on incoming damage (Forest 25%, Fort 50%, Castra 25%) — applied in `Unit.takeDamage()`.
- **Terrain `damageBonus`:** Multiplier increasing damage taken (Bridge +25%) — applied in `Unit.takeDamage()`.
- **Class `bonusAgainst`:** Flat damage addition vs specific unit classes.
- **Class `bonusAgainstLocation`:** Percentage damage bonus vs location tiles.
- **Class `receiveDamageFactorFrom`:** Damage multiplier when hit by specific attacker classes (e.g. Spearman 0.5× from Cavalry).
- **Class `damageReduction` by attack type:** Magic/siege damage reduction (e.g. Healer/Mage 50% vs magic).
- **Orc Frenzy:** `+floor(missingHp / 5)` ATK in combat.
- **Crit/variance:** Random multiplier 0.75×–1.25×.
- **Counter-attack factor:** 0.5× damage on counter-attacks, further reduced by defender's flank count (1 flank: −25%, 2: −50%, 3: −75%, 4+: suppressed).
- **Engineer 3×:** vs unoccupied fort tiles.
- **Catapult 2× tile HP:** plus skips fort damage reduction.
- **Fort HP gate:** Terrain reduction lost when tile HP = 0.
- **Deck splash:** 50% of unit damage also hits ship hull.

---

## 2. Summary: What Modifies Each Stat

| Stat | Hero base | Hero aura | Terrain | Status effects | Defend | Level-up | Combat-only |
|------|-----------|-----------|---------|----------------|--------|----------|-------------|
| **HP**  | ✓ (bonusStats) | ✓ (aura) | — | — | — | ✓ (maxHp) | Deck splash (ship hull) |
| **ATK** | ✓ (bonusStats) | ✓ (aura) | — | ✓ (bonus/reduction/mult) | — | ✓ | Frenzy, bonusAgainst, bonusAgainstLocation, flanking, crit, engineer 3× |
| **DEF** | ✓ (bonusStats) | ✓ (aura) | ✓ (defenseBonus in combat) | ✓ (defBonus) | ✓ (+2) | ✓ | — |
| **Effective toughness** | — | — | ✓ (dmgReduction, dmgBonus) | — | — | — | Class dmgReduction (magic), receiveDamageFactorFrom, fort HP gate, catapult skip reduction |
| **RNG** | — (via aura) | ✓ (aura) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **MOV** | ✓ (bonusStats) | ✓ (aura) | — | ✓ (movOverride) | — | — | — |
| **Counter** | — | — | — | — | — | — | 0.5× factor; flanked penalty (−25%/−50%/−75%/suppressed) |

Terrain also changes **effective toughness** (damage reduction/bonus) and **HP recovery** (startTurnHeal) without changing the printed DEF/HP stats.

---

## 3. Showing Players How Stats Are Modified

> **Status (Feb 27, 2026):** The first step toward stat transparency is now implemented. `HeroCardPopup.js` (new file) provides an on-demand hero info card (Option 3 style) — clicking the hero portrait in `StatPanelUI` opens a popup showing hero name, base class, portrait, level/EXP, upkeep, aura bonuses + range, ability name/description/cooldown, and bonus stats. This covers hero-specific modifiers; the full per-stat breakdown (base + aura + status + terrain + defend) for all units is still to be built per §3.3 below.

### 3.1 Approach options

#### Option 1: Hover tooltips on each stat row
- In the stat panel, hovering over a stat line (e.g. “Atk: 12 (base 5)”) shows a tooltip with the full breakdown.
- **Pros:** No layout change, discoverable, works on desktop.
- **Cons:** Weak on touch; tooltips need careful positioning.

#### Option 2: Expandable “Stat details” section in the stat panel
- A “How are these calculated?” or “Stat details” toggle under the stats that expands/collapses a block, e.g.:
  - **ATK:** 5 (base) + 2 (Marcus aura) + 4 (FORTIFY) → **11**
  - **DEF:** 4 (base) + 3 (Valeria aura) + 8 (BULWARK) + 3 (Fort) → **18**
- **Pros:** Works on all devices, no hover, all stats visible at once.
- **Cons:** Uses vertical space when expanded.

#### Option 3: Info icon / “?” per stat or per panel
- Small (i) or “?” next to the stat panel title or next to each stat. Click opens a small popover/modal with the same breakdown as in Option 2.
- **Pros:** Clean default view; breakdown on demand.
- **Cons:** One extra click; need to handle close and focus.

#### Option 4: Dedicated “Combat stats” / “Stat breakdown” tab or panel
- A separate tab or side panel (e.g. next to the unit portrait) that only shows effective stats and their sources.
- **Pros:** Room for future expansion (e.g. damage preview, terrain effects).
- **Cons:** More UI surface; might be overkill if you only need breakdowns.

---

### 3.2 Chosen approach: Option 4 — Popout card (hero-card style)

> **Decision (Feb 27, 2026):** Use a dedicated popout card, modeled on `HeroCardPopup.js`, that appears when the player clicks a stat row or a "Stats" button near the portrait. This gives enough room (360px wide, dynamic height) to show every modifier source clearly, including *which hero* provides each aura buff.

- **Trigger:** Click on any stat row in the side panel, or a dedicated icon/button near the portrait.
- **Dismiss:** Click-outside blocker (same pattern as `HeroCardPopup`).
- **Scope:** Works for **all units** (heroes, regular units, even enemy/orc units), not just heroes.

---

### 3.3 Hero aura attribution — per-stat, per-hero

Because aura bonuses **do not stack** (the best single-hero bonus wins per stat), different heroes can contribute to different stats on the same unit. The popout must show **which hero** is providing the aura bonus for **each stat individually**.

#### How it works
- `HeroManager.getAuraBonusDetail(unit, stat)` already returns `{ value, heroName }` — the winning hero's first name and the computed bonus for that specific stat.
- The call is made **per stat** (hp, atk, def, mov, rng), so a unit near two heroes could show:
  - ATK +2 (Marcus aura) — Marcus has the best ATK aura
  - DEF +3 (Valeria aura) — Valeria has the best DEF aura
  - MOV +1 (Marcus aura) — Marcus wins MOV too
- The breakdown card renders the hero name inline with each aura modifier line so the player sees exactly who is buffing what.

#### Example popout layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│         ⚔ Stat Breakdown            │
│       Knight (Lv 4) — BLUE          │
│─────────────────────────────────────│
│                                     │
│  HP: 65                             │
│    45 (base)                        │
│    +15 (Marcus — hero bonus)        │
│    +5  (Marcus — aura)              │
│                                     │
│  ATK: 15                            │
│    8 (base)                         │
│    +2 (Marcus — aura)               │
│    +4 (FORTIFY status)              │
│    +1 (level-up growth)             │
│                                     │
│  DEF: 21                            │
│    4 (base)                         │
│    +3 (Valeria — aura)              │
│    +8 (BULWARK status)              │
│    ⚔ In combat on this tile:        │
│      +3 (Fort terrain)              │
│      +2 (Defending action)          │
│                                     │
│  RNG: 1-2                           │
│    1 (base)                         │
│    +1 (Alana — aura)                │
│                                     │
│  MOV: 5                             │
│    4 (base)                         │
│    +1 (Marcus — aura)               │
│                                     │
│─────────────────────────────────────│
│  ⚔ Combat Modifiers (this unit)     │
│    Orc Frenzy: +3 ATK (15 HP miss.) │
│    Crit range: normal (no suppress) │
│    Counter dmg: 50% of normal       │
│                                     │
│  🛡 Damage Taken Modifiers           │
│    Forest: −25% damage              │
│    Magic resist: 50% less magic dmg │
│    vs Cavalry: 50% less damage      │
│    Fort tile HP: 45/100 (active)    │
│                                     │
│  ⚔ Bonus Damage (attacker)           │
│    +5 vs Archer, Healer, Mage      │
│    +100% vs Fort/Port (location)    │
│    Engineer: 3× vs Fort tiles       │
│    Catapult: 2× tile HP, skip red.  │
│                                     │
│  📍 Terrain & Position               │
│    Terrain: Forest                  │
│      25% damage reduction           │
│    Flanked: 2 sides (+25% dmg)      │
│    On deck: 50% splash to ship      │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Key points:
- Each aura line reads `+N (HeroName — aura)` so you always know *who* is providing the buff.
- If the same hero wins multiple stats, their name appears on each relevant line (e.g. Marcus on ATK **and** MOV).
- If no hero aura applies to a stat, the aura line is simply absent for that stat.
- Status effect lines show the ability/effect name (e.g. FORTIFY, BULWARK, RUNEBLAST).
- "In combat" modifiers (terrain defense bonus, Defend action) are shown in a separate sub-section under DEF, clearly labeled as combat-only since they don't change the unit's printed stat.
- **Combat Modifiers** section shows Orc Frenzy (with current bonus from missing HP), crit suppression status, and counter-attack factor.
- **Damage Taken Modifiers** shows all defender-side reductions: terrain, magic resist, class-vs-class factor, and fort tile HP status (active = tile has HP, granting reduction; destroyed = no reduction).
- **Bonus Damage** shows attacker-side additions: class bonuses, location bonuses, engineer/catapult specials.
- **Terrain & Position** shows the current tile's effects, flanking status, and deck splash info.
- Sections that have no active modifiers for the selected unit are **hidden** (e.g. Orc Frenzy only appears for orc units; magic resist only for classes that have it).

#### Color coding (modifier sources)
| Source type    | Color     | Hex       |
|----------------|-----------|-----------|
| Base stat      | White     | `#ffffff` |
| Hero aura      | Cyan      | `#88ddff` |
| Status effect  | Orange    | `#ff8844` |
| Terrain        | Blue-grey | `#88aacc` |
| Defend action  | Green     | `#88ff88` |
| Multiplier     | Gold      | `#ffd700` |
| Class bonus    | Yellow    | `#e8d070` |
| Orc/Frenzy     | Red       | `#ff6666` |

---

## 4. Implementation Plan

### Overview

Transform the existing inline `StatBreakdownPanel` (cramped 168px inside the side panel) into a full popout card matching the `HeroCardPopup` pattern. This requires changes to 4 files:

| File | Action | Scope |
|------|--------|-------|
| `managers/ui/StatBreakdown.js` | Extend | Add `type` field, add combat-only data, add `getCombatModifiers()` |
| `managers/ui/StatBreakdownPanel.js` | Rewrite | Replace inline panel with popout card renderer |
| `managers/UIManager.js` | Modify | Remove old panel from sidePanel, wire click trigger |
| `managers/ui/StatPanelUI.js` | Modify | Minor: dismiss popout on deselect/ship/location |

No changes needed to: `HeroManager.js`, `Unit.js`, `CombatManager.js`, `config.js`.

---

### Phase 1: Extend the data layer (`StatBreakdown.js`)

**Goal:** Make `getStatBreakdown()` return everything the popout card needs, including combat-only modifiers with `type` tags for color-coding.

#### Step 1.1 — Add `type` field to all modifiers

Every modifier object changes from `{ source, value }` to `{ source, value, type }` where type is one of:
`'base'`, `'aura'`, `'status'`, `'terrain'`, `'defend'`, `'multiplier'`, `'class'`, `'frenzy'`.

Changes:
- Aura modifiers: `{ source: \`${detail.heroName} aura\`, value: detail.value, type: 'aura' }`
- Status effects: `{ source: fx.type, value: fx.effect.atkBonus, type: 'status' }`
- ATK multiplier: `{ source: 'Next attack', value: \`${atkMult}×\`, type: 'multiplier' }`
- DEF combat-only (terrain): `{ source: terrain.name, value: terrain.defenseBonus, type: 'terrain' }`
- DEF combat-only (defend): `{ source: 'Defending', value: CONFIG.COMBAT.DEFEND_BONUS, type: 'defend' }`
- MOV override: `{ source: fx.type, value: '0 (frozen)', type: 'status' }`

#### Step 1.2 — Add `getCombatModifiers(scene, unit)`

New exported function returning the four context sections for the bottom of the card:

```js
/**
 * @returns {{
 *   combatMods: Array<{ label: string, type: string }>,
 *   damageTaken: Array<{ label: string, type: string }>,
 *   bonusDamage: Array<{ label: string, type: string }>,
 *   terrainPosition: Array<{ label: string, type: string }>
 * }}
 */
export function getCombatModifiers(scene, unit) { ... }
```

Data gathered:
- **combatMods:** Orc Frenzy (if `unit.team === CONFIG.TEAMS.ORC`), crit suppression (if `unit.hasNoCrit()`), counter-attack factor (always 0.5×).
- **damageTaken:** Terrain `damageReduction` on current tile, terrain `damageBonus` (Bridge), class `damageReduction` (magic/siege), `receiveDamageFactorFrom` entries, fort tile HP status (check `loc.tileHp[key] > 0`).
- **bonusDamage:** `bonusAgainst` entries (with resolved class names), `bonusAgainstLocation` entries, engineer 3× (if `engineerManager.isEngineer(unit)`), catapult specials (if `unit.classData.key === 'unit-catapult'`).
- **terrainPosition:** Current terrain name + effects, flanking count (from `combatManager.calculateFlanking(unit)`), deck splash info (if `unit.embarkedShipId && unit.isOnDeck`).

All items include a `type` field for color-coding. Sections with zero entries are returned as empty arrays (the renderer will hide them).

---

### Phase 2: Rewrite the presentation layer (`StatBreakdownPanel.js`)

**Goal:** Replace the 168px inline panel with a 360px popout card using the `HeroCardPopup` pattern.

#### Step 2.1 — New module structure

Keep the same file. Replace `create()`, `update()`, `hide()` exports with:
- `show(scene, unit)` — build and display the popout card
- `hide(scene)` — destroy the card and blocker
- `toggle(scene, unit)` — if open for same unit, hide; otherwise show

Remove: `create()` (no longer needed — card is built on demand, not pre-created).

#### Step 2.2 — Card scaffold (matches HeroCardPopup)

```js
const CARD_W = 360;
let activeCard = null;

export function show(scene, unit) {
    hide(scene);
    if (!unit || typeof unit.getEffectiveAtk !== 'function') return;

    const panelWidth = CONFIG.UI.SIDE_PANEL_WIDTH;
    const cardX = panelWidth + 12;
    const cardY = 60;

    // Full-screen invisible blocker
    const blocker = scene.add.rectangle(0, 0, CONFIG.UI.SCREEN_WIDTH, CONFIG.UI.SCREEN_HEIGHT, 0x000000, 0)
        .setOrigin(0).setScrollFactor(0).setDepth(3199).setInteractive();
    blocker.on('pointerdown', () => hide(scene));
    scene.cameras.main.ignore(blocker);
    scene._statBreakdownBlocker = blocker;

    const card = scene.add.container(cardX, cardY).setDepth(3200).setScrollFactor(0);
    card.setData('unitId', unit.id ?? `${unit.gridX},${unit.gridY}`);
    scene.cameras.main.ignore(card);

    // ... render card content (see Steps 2.3–2.5) ...

    activeCard = card;
}
```

#### Step 2.3 — Stat section rendering

For each stat (HP, ATK, DEF, RNG, MOV):

1. **Header line:** `ATK: 15` — bold white, 16px, using the effective total value.
2. **Base line:** `  8 (base)` — white, 13px, indented 12px.
3. **Modifier lines:** One per entry in `data.modifiers[]`:
   - `  +2 (Marcus — aura)` in cyan (`#88ddff`)
   - `  +4 (FORTIFY)` in orange (`#ff8844`)
   - `  2× (Next attack)` in gold (`#ffd700`)
4. **DEF combat-only sub-section:** If `data.combatOnly.length > 0`:
   - Small header: `⚔ In combat on this tile:` in blue-grey, 12px
   - One line per entry: `  +3 (Fort)`, `  +2 (Defending)`

Color looked up from `type` field via a `TYPE_COLORS` map.

Each line advances `cursorY` by its height. Stat sections separated by 8px gap.

#### Step 2.4 — Divider

After MOV, draw a horizontal line across the card (same as HeroCardPopup's section dividers):
```js
const divider = scene.add.graphics();
divider.lineStyle(1, CONFIG.UI.PANEL_TRIM, 0.5);
divider.lineBetween(20, cursorY, CARD_W - 20, cursorY);
card.add(divider);
cursorY += 10;
```

#### Step 2.5 — Combat modifier sections

Call `getCombatModifiers(scene, unit)`. For each non-empty section:

1. **Section header:** e.g. `⚔ Combat Modifiers` in gold, 14px bold.
2. **Entry lines:** Each `{ label, type }` rendered at 12px in its type color, indented 8px.
3. Skip entire section if its array is empty.

Sections in order:
- `combatMods` → `⚔ Combat Modifiers`
- `damageTaken` → `🛡 Damage Taken`
- `bonusDamage` → `⚔ Bonus Damage`
- `terrainPosition` → `📍 Terrain & Position`

#### Step 2.6 — Dynamic card background

After all content is placed, compute `finalH = cursorY + 16`. Draw the card background and hit zone exactly as HeroCardPopup does (rounded rect, panel trim, inner stroke, hit zone for pointer swallow).

---

### Phase 3: Wire the trigger (`UIManager.js`)

#### Step 3.1 — Remove old panel from sidePanel

In `setupSidePanel()`:
- Delete the `StatBreakdownPanel.create(scene, panelWidth)` call (~line 342).
- Remove `scene.statBreakdownPanel` from `scene.sidePanel.add([...])` (~line 487).

#### Step 3.2 — Add click handlers to stat text objects

After stat texts are created (~line 295), make each one interactive for click-to-popout:

```js
const statClickTargets = [scene.statHP, scene.statAtk, scene.statDef, scene.statRng, scene.statMov];
for (const obj of statClickTargets) {
    if (!obj.input) obj.setInteractive({ useHandCursor: true });
    obj.on('pointerdown', () => {
        const display = scene.selectedUnit;
        if (display && typeof display.getEffectiveAtk === 'function') {
            StatBreakdownPanel.toggle(scene, display);
        }
    });
}
```

Note: `scene.statAtk` is already interactive (for the bonus tooltip). The `pointerdown` handler for the popout will coexist with the existing `pointerover/pointerout` tooltip handlers — they use different events.

#### Step 3.3 — Add visual hint

Append a small hint text below the stats (near `scene.statMovesLeft`):
```js
scene.statBreakdownHint = scene.add.text(panelWidth / 2, statsY + rowH * 6.2, 'Click stats for details', {
    fontSize: '13px', fill: '#888888', stroke: '#000', strokeThickness: 1
}).setOrigin(0.5, 0);
```
Add to `scene.statPanel`. Only visible when a unit (not ship/location) is selected.

---

### Phase 4: Dismiss on context change (`StatPanelUI.js`)

In `updateStatPanel()`:
- When `display` is null, ship, or location tile: call `StatBreakdownPanel.hide(scene)` (already done for HeroCardPopup in the same spots — add `StatBreakdownPanel.hide(scene)` alongside).
- When switching to a non-hero unit: already calls `HeroCardPopup.hide(scene)` — `StatBreakdownPanel` stays open (auto-updates via re-trigger or the user dismisses it).

Already handled: `StatBreakdownPanel.hide(scene)` is called in the null/ship/location branches. However, the `update(scene, displayUnit)` call at ~line 131 needs to be replaced with just ensuring the popout card (if open) reflects the new unit. Simplest: if the popout is open, call `StatBreakdownPanel.show(scene, displayUnit)` to refresh it; or close it and let the user re-open.

**Decision:** Auto-refresh if open. In `updateStatPanel()`, after the unit stats are set, add:
```js
if (scene._statBreakdownBlocker) {
    StatBreakdownPanel.show(scene, displayUnit);
}
```

---

### Phase 5: Testing checklist

| # | Test case | Expected |
|---|-----------|----------|
| 1 | Click any stat row on a unit | Popout card appears to right of side panel |
| 2 | Click outside the card | Card dismisses |
| 3 | Click stat again with same unit | Card toggles off |
| 4 | Select different unit while card open | Card refreshes with new unit's data |
| 5 | Select ship/location/deselect | Card dismisses |
| 6 | Unit near 2 heroes | Different hero names on different stat aura lines |
| 7 | Unit with TESTUDO active | DEF shows +10 (TESTUDO), MOV shows clamp 2, crit suppressed |
| 8 | Orc unit at low HP | Combat Modifiers shows Frenzy bonus |
| 9 | Unit on Fort with HP > 0 | Terrain section shows 50% reduction, DEF combat-only shows +3 |
| 10 | Unit on Fort with tile HP = 0 | Terrain section shows "Fort (destroyed — no reduction)" |
| 11 | Unit on Bridge | Damage Taken shows +25% |
| 12 | Knight selected | Bonus Damage shows +5 vs Archer/Healer/Mage |
| 13 | Catapult selected | Bonus Damage shows +100% vs Fort/Port, 2× tile HP |
| 14 | Spearman selected | Damage Taken shows 50% less from Cavalry |
| 15 | Hero portrait click | Hero card popup (unchanged) |
| 16 | Click stat while hero card open | Hero card closes, stat card opens |
| 17 | Unit on deck | Terrain & Position shows deck splash |
| 18 | Unit with no modifiers | Card shows just base values, no bottom sections |

---

### File change summary

| File | Lines changed (est.) | Type |
|------|---------------------|------|
| `managers/ui/StatBreakdown.js` | +60 (add types + `getCombatModifiers`) | Extend |
| `managers/ui/StatBreakdownPanel.js` | ~200 (full rewrite) | Rewrite |
| `managers/UIManager.js` | ~15 (remove old panel, add click handlers + hint) | Modify |
| `managers/ui/StatPanelUI.js` | ~5 (replace `update` call, add auto-refresh) | Modify |
| **Total** | ~280 lines | |
